Professor View 01 / 40
Travel English -- 40 Aulas

Pricila Adamo

De "eu sei o que quero dizer mas não consigo dizer" para "passaporte pronto, inglês pronto" -- falar com alegria, não com esforço, em 40 aulas

B1+ (B2 operacional) Araras, SP Dentista em transição para aposentadoria 60 min / Online
Progresso Geral0%
Velocidade:
Pricila Adamo
50 anos
Dentista em transição para aposentadoria
Araras, SP (trânsito frequente em São Paulo)
B1+ (B2 operacional)
Fluência conversacional para viagens e vida internacional
40 aulas de 60 min (3x/semana)
Online (Zoom)

Jornada de Transformação

De: "Falo inglês mas travo na hora de detalhar — sei o que quero dizer, mas na hora de organizar as coisas, fica pior. Fico cansada de estudar e quero só ter alegria."

Para: "Estou sempre pronta — se aparecer uma viagem, se aparecer um estrangeiro, se aparecer um filme sem legenda. O inglês virou parte da minha rotina, não uma obrigação." em 40 aulas

Promessa do Programa

"Eu gosto de estar aqui em passaporte, com tudo pronto." — De 'eu sei o que quero dizer mas não consigo dizer' para 'passaporte pronto, inglês pronto' — falar com alegria, não com esforço, em 40 aulas.

Forças

  • Ausência de vergonha comunicativa — fala sem filtro, mesmo errando
  • Iniciativa de imersão autônoma — cria micro-hábitos em inglês por conta própria
  • Alto engajamento temático — conecta o inglês a interesses genuínos
  • Autoconsciência linguística — sabe identificar seus gaps com precisão
  • Experiência real com o idioma — viagens internacionais, atendimento de pacientes anglófonos
  • Energia emocional estável e animada

Pontos de Melhoria

  • Densidade informacional em inglês — respostas curtas, perde detalhes
  • Estrutura sintática — frases incompletas, autocorreção frequente
  • Vocabulário ativo limitado — compreende mais do que produz
  • Compreensão auditiva de sotaques variados
  • Consistência na rotina de estudos — histórico de ciclos de abandono
  • Fadiga cognitiva ao estudar inglês

Currículo -- 40 Aulas

#TemaFoco LinguísticoAtividade PrincipalHomework
1Who Is Pricila Adamo? -- Diagnostic + Personal StoryPresent simple, past simple, present perfect (diagnostic)Personal timeline + diagnostic conversationRecord 3-min audio telling your story
2My Life in Chapters -- Past ExperiencesPast simple vs present perfectLife chapters narrativeWrite 5 sentences about last week
3The Retirement Dream -- Plans and GoalsGoing to, will, present continuous for futureVision board discussionRecord your retirement plans
4A Life of Travel -- Describing PlacesAdjective order, comparatives, superlativesTravel photo storytellingDescribe your favorite trip
5Health and Wellness -- Self-Care LanguageModal verbs (should, could, might)Wellness routine role-playRecord daily routine in English
6At the Airport -- Checking In and BoardingPolite requests (Could I...?, Would you mind...?)Airport check-in simulationWatch airport scene from a film
7Hotel Check-in -- Reservations and RequestsWould like to, need to, have toHotel role-play with problemsWrite a hotel complaint email
8Getting Around -- TransportationGiving and following directions, prepositions of placeNavigation challengeDescribe directions to your favorite place
9Eating Out -- Restaurants and Dietary NeedsOrdering language, dietary vocabularyRestaurant role-playRecord ordering a meal
10Shopping Abroad -- Markets and BargainingQuantifiers, prices, too/enoughMarket negotiationDescribe your last shopping experience
11Small Talk -- Conversation with StrangersQuestion forms, tag questionsSpeed-dating style conversationsStart 3 conversations in English this week
12Sharing Opinions -- Agreeing and DisagreeingOpinion frames, hedging languageDebate on travel topicsRecord opinion on a podcast episode
13Telling Stories -- Narrative SkillsPast continuous, time sequencersStorytelling challenge (Canada hospital)Write your Canada hospital story
14Cultural Differences -- Comparing CustomsUsed to, would (past habits)Cross-cultural comparison discussionCompare Brazilian and Australian customs
15Making Friends Abroad -- Building ConnectionsPhrasal verbs for relationshipsSocial event role-playWrite a message to a new friend
16Medical Situations Abroad -- Describing SymptomsPresent perfect continuous, body vocabularyHospital emergency role-playRecord describing a health issue
17Money Matters -- Banking and PaymentsPassive voice (basic), financial vocabularyBank and currency exchange role-playExplain your travel budget
18Technology and Gadgets -- Digital LifeRelative clauses, tech vocabularyTech support conversationDescribe your Oura Ring in English
19Renting and Driving -- Cars and NavigationConditional type 1 (if + present, will)Car rental role-playRecord directions for a road trip
20Midterm Review -- Integration CheckAll structures from lessons 1-19Full travel simulation (airport to hotel)Self-assessment recording
21Discover Australia -- Geography and CulturePassive voice, geographical vocabularyAustralia quiz and discussionResearch 3 Australian cities
22Australian English -- Slang and AccentInformal register, Australian expressionsAussie dialogue comprehensionWatch Australian YouTube video
23The Great Barrier Reef -- Nature and EnvironmentEnvironmental vocabulary, conditionals type 2Eco-tourism debateRecord opinion on nature conservation
24City Life -- Sydney, Melbourne, BeyondRecommendations (You should, I would suggest)City guide presentationPlan a 3-day Sydney itinerary
25Outback Adventures -- ActivitiesGerunds and infinitivesAdventure booking role-playDescribe your dream adventure
26Movies Without Subtitles -- Film DiscussionReview language, adjectives for filmsFilm review and discussionWatch Eat Pray Love scene without subtitles
27Podcasts and Audiobooks -- Listening SkillsReported speech (basic)Podcast summary and discussionListen to Hidden Brain episode
28History in English -- Discussing EventsPast perfect, time clausesHistorical timeline discussionRecord summary of a historical event
29Psychology in English -- Behavior and MindAbstract vocabulary, cause and effectPsychology TED Talk discussionDescribe a personality trait in English
30Technology Trends -- InnovationFuture forms review, predictionsTech debateRecord tech prediction
31Debates and Discussions -- Defending ViewsDiscourse markers, concession languageFormal debateWrite argument for and against
32Emotional Intelligence -- Complex FeelingsVocabulary for emotions, wish + pastEmotional scenario role-playsDescribe a challenging emotion in English
33The Art of Persuasion -- ConvincingPersuasive language, rhetorical questionsPersuasion challengeRecord persuasive pitch
34Problem-Solving Abroad -- Unexpected SituationsThird conditional, regret languageCrisis simulationWrite about a travel problem you solved
35Hosting and Being a Guest -- Social EventsSocial register, invitationsDinner party role-playWrite a dinner invitation email
36Travel Planning -- Australia ItineraryAll future forms, planning languageComplete itinerary presentationFinalize your Australia itinerary
37At Immigration -- Confidence Under PressureQuestion handling, formal registerImmigration interview simulationPractice immigration Q&A
38Your English Portfolio -- Reviewing ProgressSelf-assessment language, reflectionProgress presentationRecord before/after comparison
39Full Immersion Day -- Travel SimulationAll structures integrated90-min immersion: airport to dinnerReflection journal
40Graduation -- Celebrating and Next StepsFuture plans, celebration languageFinal presentation + celebrationLetter to future self in English

Welcome, Pricila!

Onboarding

Bem-vinda ao programa! Antes de cada aula, complete as atividades de Pre-class. Aqui estão 5 frases de emergência que você pode usar a qualquer momento:

1 Could you repeat that, please? Poderia repetir, por favor?
2 I am not sure I understand. Could you explain? Não tenho certeza se entendi. Poderia explicar?
3 Let me think about that for a moment. Deixe-me pensar um momento.
4 To be honest, I have always wanted to... Para ser honesta, eu sempre quis...
5 I have been studying English for many years. Estudo inglês há muitos anos.
Aula 1 -- Pre-class

Reintroducing Myself at B2

Conte a sua historia com vocabulario rico (turning point, milestone, thriving) e fale do que voce vem fazendo com o present perfect continuous (I have been + -ing).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Your Story

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for telling the story of your life and your reinvention.

A career path -- trajetoria profissional
"My career path has taken several unexpected turns."
A turning point -- um ponto de virada
"Retiring became a real turning point for me."
To take stock -- fazer um balanco / refletir
"I have been taking stock of what really matters."
Fulfilling -- gratificante / que da sentido
"Learning English is the most fulfilling thing I do."
To look back on -- relembrar / olhar para tras
"I look back on my career with real pride."
A milestone -- um marco importante
"Opening my own clinic was a huge milestone."
To be passionate about -- ser apaixonado por
"I have always been passionate about travel."
To come a long way -- evoluir muito / progredir bastante
"My English has come a long way this year."
To juggle -- conciliar varias coisas ao mesmo tempo
"I have been juggling new hobbies and travel plans."
Demanding -- exigente / que pede muito esforco
"Dentistry was a demanding but rewarding career."
A fresh perspective -- um novo olhar / perspectiva renovada
"Travel has given me a fresh perspective on life."
To thrive -- prosperar / florescer
"She has been thriving since she retired."
Well-rounded -- completo / com interesses variados
"I want to be a more well-rounded person in this chapter."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

A turning point
To take stock
Fulfilling
To look back on
A milestone
Passionate about
To come a long way
To juggle
Demanding
A fresh perspective
To thrive
Well-rounded

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's reintroduction and answer the questions.

"Let me reintroduce myself. For most of my life I was a dentist -- a demanding but rewarding career path. Recently I reached a real turning point, and I have been taking stock of what truly matters. These days I have been juggling travel, languages and new interests, and honestly, learning English has been the most fulfilling part of all. When I look back on my milestones, I feel proud -- but I have come a long way, and I genuinely feel I am only just getting started."

1. What does Pricila say she has been doing recently?
A Taking stock of what truly matters.
B Going back to full-time dentistry.
C Avoiding any new activities.
2. Which structure does she use for ongoing recent actions?
A Past simple ("I worked").
B Present perfect continuous ("I have been juggling").
C Future ("I will juggle").
3. How does Pricila feel about her journey?
A She regrets her career.
B She is proud and feels she is just getting started.
C She feels her best years are over.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect Continuous

Grammar

Para falar de uma acao que comecou no passado e continua ate agora (muitas vezes com for / since), use have / has + been + verbo-ing. Ela destaca a DURACAO e o esforco recente. / To talk about an action that started in the past and is still going on (often with for / since), use have / has + been + verb-ing. It highlights the duration and recent effort.

Forma / FormEnglishPortugues
AffirmativeI have been learning English for a year.Estudo ingles ha um ano.
Affirmative (he/she)She has been thriving since she retired.Ela vem prosperando desde que se aposentou.
NegativeI have not been wasting my time.Eu nao venho perdendo tempo.
InterrogativeWhat have you been doing lately?O que voce vem fazendo ultimamente?
Common mistakeNOT "I study English since 2000." Say "I have been studying since 2000."Com since / for, use have been + -ing.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"I taking stock of my life."
"Retiring was a real turning ."
"My English has come a long ."
"What have you doing lately?"
"Learning English is the most thing I do."
"Travel has given me a fresh on life."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's reintroduction in order.

?These days, she has been juggling travel, languages and new interests.
?Finally, she says she has come a long way and feels she is just getting started.
?First, she explains that dentistry was a long and demanding career path.
?After that, she calls learning English the most fulfilling part of all.
?Then she reaches a turning point and starts taking stock of what matters.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the natural contraction "I've been".

I have been taking stock of my life.
Tenho feito um balanco da minha vida.
Retiring became a real turning point for me.
Me aposentar virou um verdadeiro ponto de virada.
My English has come a long way.
Meu ingles evoluiu muito.
I am still curious and well-rounded.
Continuo curiosa e com interesses variados.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

An old colleague asks what you have been doing since you stepped back from the clinic. What do you say?
A "I have been juggling travel, languages and new hobbies."
B "I will do nothing forever."
C "I did dentistry yesterday."
You want to describe a moment that changed the direction of your life. Which phrase fits?
A "It was a small daily routine."
B "It was a real turning point for me."
C "It was a demanding mistake."
Someone assumes you have stopped growing. How do you correct them politely?
A "Yes, my best years are over."
B "Actually, I have come a long way and I am still thriving."
C "I never learn anything new."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Reintroduce yourself at a B2 level in 5-6 sentences. Mention one milestone you look back on with pride, one turning point in your life, and what you have been doing lately. Use at least three present perfect continuous forms ("I have been..."). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 1

1I have been taking stock of my life.Tenho feito um balanco da minha vida.
2Retiring was a real turning point.Me aposentar foi um verdadeiro ponto de virada.
3My English has come a long way.Meu ingles evoluiu muito.
4I have been juggling travel and new hobbies.Tenho conciliado viagens e novos hobbies.
5I am still thriving and just getting started.Continuo prosperando e so estou comecando.
Aula 2 -- Pre-class

Chapters & Turning Points

Conte a historia POR TRAS da sua vida com vocabulario rico (setback, leap of faith, defining moment) e narre o passado com o past perfect (had + particicio) para a acao que veio antes.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Life's Journey

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These B2 expressions help you tell the story behind your biggest decisions.

A setback -- um reves / contratempo
"Losing the contract was a real setback for us."
To overcome -- superar
"She overcame every obstacle in her way."
A pivotal moment -- um momento decisivo
"That meeting was a pivotal moment in my career."
In hindsight -- em retrospecto / olhando para tras
"In hindsight, leaving was the best choice I ever made."
To embark on -- embarcar em / iniciar algo novo
"At fifty, she embarked on a whole new career."
Daunting -- assustador / intimidador
"Starting again felt daunting at first."
A leap of faith -- um salto de fe / decisao arriscada
"Selling the house was a real leap of faith."
To pave the way -- abrir caminho
"That first trip paved the way for everything after."
A gut feeling -- uma intuicao / pressentimento
"I had a gut feeling it was time to go."
Against all odds -- contra todas as probabilidades
"Against all odds, the new bakery thrived."
To put behind you -- deixar para tras / superar
"She put the difficult years behind her."
A defining moment -- um momento que define quem voce e
"That decision was a defining moment for me."
Rewarding -- gratificante / recompensador
"The new chapter was far more rewarding."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

A setback
To overcome
A pivotal moment
In hindsight
To embark on
Daunting
A leap of faith
To pave the way
A gut feeling
To put behind you
A defining moment
Rewarding

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story of Pricila's turning point and answer the questions. Notice which action happened first.

By the time Pricila finally decided to step back from the clinic, she had been dreaming of change for years. The early days had brought more than one setback, and at first the idea felt daunting. But she had always trusted her gut feeling. In hindsight, selling part of the practice was a real leap of faith -- yet it paved the way for a far more rewarding chapter. When she looked back, she realized that one quiet decision had become the defining moment of her whole life.

1. Which action happened FIRST?
A She had been dreaming of change (before she decided to step back).
B She decided to step back, then started dreaming.
C Both happened at the exact same time.
2. What tense marks the earlier of two past actions here?
A Present perfect ("has decided").
B Past perfect ("had become", "had been dreaming").
C Future ("will become").
3. How does Pricila describe that quiet decision?
A As a forgettable mistake.
B As the defining moment of her life.
C As a setback she never overcame.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Past Perfect (Narrative Tenses)

Grammar

Quando duas acoes acontecem no passado, use o past perfect (had + particicio) para a que veio ANTES, e o past simple para a que veio depois. Para uma acao anterior que estava em andamento, use had been + verbo-ing. / When two actions happen in the past, use the past perfect (had + past participle) for the EARLIER one, and the past simple for the later one. For an earlier ongoing action, use had been + verb-ing.

Forma / FormEnglishPortugues
Past perfect (earlier action)By the time I arrived, she had left.Quando cheguei, ela ja tinha saido.
Past perfect + past simpleShe opened a new shop after she had sold the old one.Ela abriu uma loja nova depois de ter vendido a antiga.
Past perfect continuousI had been dreaming of change for years before I left.Eu vinha sonhando com mudanca havia anos antes de sair.
NegativeI had not realized how much I needed it.Eu nao tinha percebido o quanto eu precisava daquilo.
Common mistakeNOT "When I arrived, she left." (if she left first) -- say "she had left".A acao anterior leva had + particicio.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"By the time I arrived, she ."
"I dreaming of change for years."
"That first trip paved the for everything after."
"Selling the house was a real leap of ."
", leaving was the best choice I ever made."
", the new bakery thrived."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put the chapters of Pricila's turning point in order.

?Trusting a gut feeling, she took a leap of faith and stepped back from the clinic.
?In hindsight, that quiet decision had become the defining moment of her life.
?For years, she had been dreaming of a change she could not yet name.
?Against all odds, the move paved the way for a far more rewarding chapter.
?The early setbacks had felt daunting, and she nearly gave up.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself. Notice the contraction "she'd left" and the stress in "daunting" and "pivotal".

By the time I arrived, she had left.
Quando cheguei, ela ja tinha saido.
It was a defining moment for me.
Foi um momento decisivo para mim.
I had a gut feeling it was time to go.
Tive um pressentimento de que era hora de ir.
Against all odds, I started again.
Contra todas as probabilidades, recomecei.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to describe a brave, risky decision you made. Which phrase fits?
A "It was a real leap of faith, but I took it."
B "It was completely safe and obvious."
C "I never made any decisions."
You are telling a story where one past action happened before another. How do you mark the earlier one?
A "When I arrived, she leaves."
B "When I arrived, she had already left."
C "When I arrived, she will leave."
A friend asks how you feel about a difficult period that is now over. What sounds natural?
A "It still controls everything I do."
B "I have finally put those years behind me."
C "Nothing ever happened to me."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Tell the story behind one turning point in your life. Describe a setback you had overcome before it, the leap of faith you took, and why it became a defining moment. Use the past perfect at least twice ("By the time... I had..."). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 2

1By the time I decided, I had been dreaming of it for years.Quando decidi, eu ja vinha sonhando com isso havia anos.
2It was a real leap of faith.Foi um verdadeiro salto de fe.
3In hindsight, it was the best decision.Em retrospecto, foi a melhor decisao.
4That choice paved the way for everything after.Aquela escolha abriu caminho para tudo depois.
5I have finally put those years behind me.Finalmente deixei aqueles anos para tras.
Aula 3 -- Pre-class

The Retirement Dream

Fale das suas metas e da vida pela frente com vocabulario rico (bucket list, aspiration, seize the moment) e projete o futuro com o future continuous (will be + -ing) e o future perfect (will have + particicio).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Your Goals

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These B2 expressions help you talk about the dreams and goals ahead of you.

A bucket list -- lista de coisas para fazer antes de morrer
"Walking the Camino is top of my bucket list."
To set your sights on -- mirar firmemente em uma meta
"I have set my sights on visiting Japan next year."
To make the most of -- aproveitar ao maximo
"I want to make the most of this fresh chapter."
On the horizon -- no horizonte / prestes a acontecer
"With a big trip on the horizon, I feel excited."
Long-overdue -- atrasado / que ja devia ter acontecido
"This long-overdue holiday is finally happening."
An aspiration -- uma aspiracao / ambicao
"Learning three languages is a lifelong aspiration."
To broaden your horizons -- ampliar seus horizontes
"Travel always broadens my horizons."
To seize the moment -- aproveitar a oportunidade / agarrar o momento
"I decided to seize the moment and book the flight."
A fresh start -- um recomeco
"This year feels like a true fresh start."
Uncharted -- inexplorado / desconhecido
"Retirement felt like uncharted territory at first."
To map out -- planejar em detalhe
"Let's map out the whole journey together."
Attainable -- alcancavel / realista
"Set an attainable goal, then plan backwards."
To look forward to -- aguardar com expectativa / ansiar por
"I'm really looking forward to the year ahead."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

A bucket list
To set your sights on
To make the most of
On the horizon
Long-overdue
An aspiration
To broaden your horizons
To seize the moment
A fresh start
Uncharted
To map out
Attainable

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read about Pricila's plans and answer the questions. Notice how she describes the future.

Pricila has finally set her sights on a long-overdue dream. "This time next year, I will be travelling through Japan," she says, smiling. By the time she boards the plane, she will have finished her English course and mapped out the whole trip. It is top of her bucket list, and with that adventure on the horizon, she is determined to make the most of every day. Retirement once felt like uncharted territory; now it feels like a fresh start she is truly looking forward to.

1. Which sentence describes an action IN PROGRESS at a future time?
A "This time next year, I will be travelling through Japan."
B "I travelled to Japan last year."
C "I have never been to Japan."
2. Which sentence describes an action FINISHED before a future point?
A "I am studying English now."
B "By the time she boards, she will have finished her course."
C "She finishes her course."
3. How does Pricila feel about retirement now?
A Like frightening, uncharted territory she avoids.
B Like a fresh start she is looking forward to.
C Like the end of her best years.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Future Continuous & Future Perfect

Grammar

Para uma acao que estara EM ANDAMENTO num momento futuro, use o future continuous: will be + verbo-ing. Para uma acao que estara CONCLUIDA antes de um momento futuro, use o future perfect: will have + particicio. / For an action IN PROGRESS at a future moment, use the future continuous (will be + verb-ing). For an action COMPLETED before a future point, use the future perfect (will have + past participle).

Forma / FormEnglishPortugues
Future continuous (in progress)This time next year, I will be travelling.No ano que vem, eu estarei viajando.
Future perfect (completed)By next year, I will have reached my goal.Ate o ano que vem, eu ja terei alcancado minha meta.
NegativeI will not have finished the trip by June.Nao terei terminado a viagem ate junho.
InterrogativeWill you be living abroad this time next year?Voce estara morando fora no ano que vem?
Common mistakeNOT "This time next year I will travel." Say "I will be travelling" (in progress).Acao em andamento no futuro = will be + -ing.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"This time next year, I will travelling."
"By next year, I will reached my goal."
"I have set my on visiting Japan."
"It is top of my bucket ."
"I want to make the most every day."
"I'm really looking to the year ahead."

Stage 2: Order the Plan

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's plan for the year ahead in order.

?Next, she maps out an attainable plan and sets her sights on Japan.
?This time next year, she will be travelling and broadening her horizons.
?First, retirement opens a long-overdue fresh start.
?By the time she boards, she will have finished her English course.
?Then she writes the dreams that are top of her bucket list.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself. Notice "aspiration" (as-puh-RAY-shun) and the contraction "I'll have".

This time next year, I will be travelling.
No ano que vem, eu estarei viajando.
By next year, I will have reached my goal.
Ate o ano que vem, ja terei alcancado minha meta.
I have set my sights on visiting Japan.
Mirei firmemente em visitar o Japao.
It is top of my bucket list.
Esta no topo da minha lista de desejos.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to say you will be in the middle of a trip at a future moment. What fits?
A "This time next month, I will be exploring Kyoto."
B "Last month I explored Kyoto."
C "I explore Kyoto every day."
You want to say a task will be complete before a deadline. What sounds natural?
A "By June, I am finishing the course."
B "By June, I will have finished the course."
C "By June, I finished the course yesterday."
A friend asks about your dreams for the years ahead. Which answer fits the topic best?
A "I have no plans and want nothing."
B "I want to seize the moment and broaden my horizons."
C "I only think about the past."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe your dream for the year ahead. Name one item at the top of your bucket list, one aspiration you have set your sights on, and what you will be doing this time next year. Use the future continuous ("I will be...") and the future perfect ("By then, I will have...") at least once each. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 3

1This time next year, I will be travelling.No ano que vem, eu estarei viajando.
2By then, I will have reached my goal.Ate la, eu ja terei alcancado minha meta.
3It is top of my bucket list.Esta no topo da minha lista de desejos.
4I want to seize the moment and make the most of it.Quero agarrar o momento e aproveitar ao maximo.
5I'm really looking forward to the year ahead.Estou muito ansiosa pelo ano que vem.
Aula 4 -- Pre-class

A Life of Travel

Descreva lugares com vocabulario vivo (bustling, hidden gem, awe-inspiring) e faca deducoes sobre eles com os modais de probabilidade (must be / might be / can't be, e must have been + particicio).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words to Describe the World

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These B2 adjectives and expressions paint a vivid picture of any place.

Bustling -- agitado / cheio de vida
"The bustling market was alive with vendors and music."
Unspoiled -- intocado / preservado
"We found an unspoiled beach with no hotels in sight."
A hidden gem -- uma joia escondida / lugar especial pouco conhecido
"That tiny restaurant is a real hidden gem."
A landmark -- um marco / ponto de referencia famoso
"The cathedral is the city's most famous landmark."
Off the beaten track -- fora dos roteiros turisticos
"We like villages off the beaten track, away from the crowds."
Awe-inspiring -- deslumbrante / que inspira admiracao
"The view from the top was absolutely awe-inspiring."
Sprawling -- vasto / espalhado por uma area enorme
"Mexico City is a sprawling metropolis that never ends."
Picturesque -- pitoresco / encantador como um cartao-postal
"The picturesque fishing village looked like a postcard."
Overrated -- superestimado / menos impressionante do que dizem
"Honestly, the famous tower was a bit overrated."
To immerse yourself -- mergulhar / se envolver totalmente
"I love to immerse myself in the local food and customs."
To be worth the trip -- valer a viagem
"The road was long, but the ruins were worth the trip."
A getaway -- uma escapada / viagem curta para relaxar
"A quiet weekend getaway in the mountains is just what I need."
To stand out -- se destacar
"Of all the cities I have seen, Kyoto really stands out."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Bustling
Unspoiled
A hidden gem
A landmark
Off the beaten track
Awe-inspiring
Sprawling
Picturesque
Overrated
To immerse yourself
To be worth the trip
To stand out

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read about Pricila looking at travel photos and answer the questions. Notice how she guesses about each place.

Pricila is sorting old photos. "Look at this crowd -- it must be a famous landmark," she says. The next photo shows an empty shore. "It's so quiet, it might be a beach off the beaten track." Then a grey, dull street: "There's nothing special here -- it can't be the picturesque village everyone talks about." Finally, a faded image of a temple. "This must have been awe-inspiring in its day. What a hidden gem -- definitely worth the trip."

1. Which phrase shows Pricila is almost SURE about something?
A "It must be a famous landmark."
B "It might be a beach."
C "It can't be the village."
2. Which phrase means she is SURE something is NOT true?
A "It must be a landmark."
B "It can't be the picturesque village."
C "It might be a quiet beach."
3. How does she make a deduction about the PAST?
A "It must be awe-inspiring."
B "This must have been awe-inspiring in its day."
C "This will be awe-inspiring."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Modals of Deduction

Grammar

Para fazer deducoes (adivinhar com base em pistas), use must be (quase certeza de que e verdade), might/could be (e possivel) e can't be (certeza de que NAO e). Para o passado, use must have been / might have been / can't have been + particicio. / To make deductions (guessing from evidence), use must be (almost sure it's true), might/could be (it's possible) and can't be (sure it's NOT true). For the past, use must have been / might have been / can't have been + past participle.

Certeza / CertaintyEnglishPortugues
Almost sure (90%)With those crowds, it must be a famous landmark.Com essa multidao, deve ser um marco famoso.
Possible (50%)It's so empty, it might be off the beaten track.Esta tao vazio que pode ser fora dos roteiros.
Sure it's NOT trueThere's no one here, it can't be a tourist hotspot.Nao tem ninguem aqui, nao pode ser ponto turistico.
Past deductionThis old photo -- the place must have been awe-inspiring.Essa foto antiga -- o lugar deve ter sido deslumbrante.
Common mistakeNOT "It must be beautiful yesterday." Say "It must have been beautiful."Deducao no passado = must have been + particicio.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"With those crowds, it be a famous landmark."
"It's so empty, it be off the beaten track."
"There's no one here, it be a tourist hotspot."
"This old photo -- the place must have awe-inspiring."
"That tiny restaurant is a real hidden ."
"The road was long, but the ruins were worth the ."

Stage 2: Order the Description

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's description of her favorite trip in order.

?Beyond the crowds, she found an unspoiled, picturesque village off the beaten track.
?Of all her trips, that hidden gem still stands out as worth the trip.
?First, she arrived in a bustling, sprawling city full of life.
?There she immersed herself in the local food and customs.
?The famous landmark, though, was a bit overrated.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself. Notice the reduced sound of "must've been".

With those crowds, it must be a famous landmark.
Com essa multidao, deve ser um marco famoso.
The place must have been awe-inspiring.
O lugar deve ter sido deslumbrante.
It was a real hidden gem, worth the trip.
Era uma verdadeira joia escondida, valeu a viagem.
I love to immerse myself in the local culture.
Adoro mergulhar na cultura local.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You see a long line and crowds outside a building. You are almost sure why. What do you say?
A "It must be a famous landmark."
B "It can't be popular at all."
C "It will be empty."
A friend asks why you loved a quiet village. Which answer fits the theme best?
A "It was crowded and overrated."
B "It was an unspoiled hidden gem, off the beaten track."
C "It was just a normal city street."
Looking at an old photo of a ruined temple, how do you make a deduction about its past?
A "It must be awe-inspiring tomorrow."
B "It must have been awe-inspiring in its day."
C "It can be awe-inspiring last year."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe one place that really stands out from your travels. Use at least four describing words (bustling, unspoiled, awe-inspiring, picturesque, a hidden gem...) and make one deduction about it with "It must have been..." or "It can't be...". Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 4

1It must be a famous landmark.Deve ser um marco famoso.
2The view was absolutely awe-inspiring.A vista era absolutamente deslumbrante.
3It was a real hidden gem.Era uma verdadeira joia escondida.
4Honestly, it was a bit overrated.Sinceramente, foi um pouco superestimado.
5It was off the beaten track, but worth the trip.Era fora dos roteiros, mas valeu a viagem.
Aula 5 -- Pre-class

Wellbeing & Resilience

Fale sobre estresse, descanso e recuperacao com vocabulario B2 (burnout, downtime, resilience) e expresse arrependimento sobre o passado com should have / ought to have + participio passado, alem de conselhos com had better.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Wellbeing

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for talking about stress, rest and bouncing back.

Burnout -- esgotamento fisico e mental
"After two years with no break, she was close to burnout."
To unwind -- relaxar / aliviar a tensao
"I unwind in the evening with a quiet walk and some tea."
To recharge -- recarregar as energias
"A weekend with no screens is how I recharge."
Downtime -- tempo livre para descansar
"I now treat my downtime as seriously as my work."
Overwhelmed -- sobrecarregado / sem conta com tudo
"With three deadlines at once, I felt completely overwhelmed."
To set boundaries -- estabelecer limites
"Learning to set boundaries at work changed everything for me."
To slow down -- desacelerar
"My doctor told me firmly that I needed to slow down."
Self-care -- autocuidado
"For years I saw self-care as selfish -- now I know it is essential."
Resilience -- resiliencia / forca para se recuperar
"Resilience is not avoiding stress; it is recovering well from it."
To bounce back -- se recuperar rapidamente
"It took months, but she bounced back stronger than before."
Sustainable habits -- habitos que se mantem por anos
"Small, sustainable habits beat one dramatic change every time."
Work-life balance -- equilibrio entre trabalho e vida
"Since I retired part-time, my work-life balance has finally improved."
To keep up with -- acompanhar o ritmo de
"I exhausted myself trying to keep up with everyone else's schedule."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Burnout
To unwind
To recharge
Downtime
Overwhelmed
To set boundaries
To slow down
Self-care
Resilience
To bounce back
Sustainable habits
Work-life balance
To keep up with

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's reflection and answer the questions.

"For years I pushed myself far too hard. Looking back, I should have set boundaries much sooner, and I certainly ought to have taken more downtime. I ignored the early signs of burnout -- I was constantly overwhelmed, trying to keep up with everyone else. My doctor was blunt: 'You had better slow down, or your health will pay the price.' She was right. These days I protect my downtime, I build small sustainable habits, and I treat self-care as essential, not selfish. I still have stressful weeks, but I bounce back far more quickly now."

1. How does Pricila feel about the past?
A She should have set boundaries much sooner.
B She is proud that she never rested.
C She regrets slowing down at all.
2. What strong advice did her doctor give?
A "You should have relaxed yesterday."
B "You had better slow down."
C "You will slow down next year."
3. Which structure expresses regret or criticism about the past?
A Present continuous ("I am setting boundaries").
B should have + past participle ("I should have set").
C Future ("I will set boundaries").

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- should / ought to have + past participle

Grammar

Para falar de um arrependimento ou uma critica sobre o passado (voce fez ou nao fez algo, e hoje ve que era o certo), use should have / ought to have + participio passado. Para um conselho forte ou aviso sobre o presente / futuro proximo, use had better + verbo base (sem have). / To talk about a regret or criticism about the past, use should have / ought to have + past participle. For strong advice or a warning about now or the near future, use had better + base verb (no have).

Forma / FormEnglishPortugues
Regret (affirmative)I should have slowed down earlier.Eu deveria ter desacelerado antes.
Regret (ought to)She ought to have set boundaries.Ela deveria ter estabelecido limites.
NegativeI should not have ignored the signs.Eu nao deveria ter ignorado os sinais.
Advice now (had better)You had better rest this weekend.E melhor voce descansar neste fim de semana.
Common mistakeNOT "I should slowed down." Say "I should have slowed down."Sempre have + participio passado.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"I should set boundaries much sooner."
"You had slow down before you burn out."
"For years I saw self- as selfish."
"Small, habits beat one dramatic change."
"It took months, but she back stronger."
"I exhausted myself trying to keep with everyone."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's wellbeing story in order.

?After that, she started to set boundaries and protect her downtime.
?Finally, she says she now bounces back from stress far more quickly.
?First, she admits she pushed herself too hard and ignored the signs of burnout.
?She built small, sustainable habits and stopped seeing self-care as selfish.
?Then her doctor warned her that she had better slow down.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the linking in "should have" (sounds like "should've").

I should have set boundaries much sooner.
Eu deveria ter estabelecido limites muito antes.
I now treat my downtime as seriously as my work.
Agora levo meu tempo de descanso tao a serio quanto meu trabalho.
Resilience is recovering well, not avoiding stress.
Resiliencia e se recuperar bem, nao evitar o estresse.
Small sustainable habits keep me balanced.
Pequenos habitos sustentaveis me mantem equilibrada.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

A friend tells you she feels close to burnout. What is a supportive B2 response?
A "You had better set some boundaries and protect your downtime."
B "Just work harder and you will feel fine."
C "Burnout is not a real thing."
You want to say you recover quickly after a hard week. Which phrase fits?
A "I never feel any stress at all."
B "I have learned to bounce back and build sustainable habits."
C "I keep up with everyone until I collapse."
You regret not resting earlier in life. How do you express it?
A "I will slow down someday, maybe."
B "I should have slowed down long before my body forced me to."
C "I am slowing down right now as we speak."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Talk for 5-6 sentences about your own wellbeing. Describe one thing you should have done differently in the past (use "should have + past participle"), one boundary you now set, and one sustainable habit that helps you recharge. Use at least two "should have" forms and the word resilience. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 5

1I should have set boundaries much sooner.Eu deveria ter estabelecido limites muito antes.
2You had better slow down and recharge.E melhor voce desacelerar e recarregar as energias.
3I treat self-care as essential, not selfish.Trato o autocuidado como essencial, nao egoismo.
4Small sustainable habits keep me balanced.Pequenos habitos sustentaveis me mantem equilibrada.
5I bounce back from stress much faster now.Me recupero do estresse muito mais rapido agora.
Aula 6 -- Pre-class

Smooth Travels: At the Airport

Resolva imprevistos no aeroporto com vocabulario B2 (overbooked, layover, rebooked, baggage claim) e conte o que a equipe te disse usando reported speech (She said... / They told me...).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for the Airport

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for handling problems at the airport.

Overbooked -- com mais reservas que assentos
"The flight was overbooked, so they asked for volunteers to give up their seats."
A layover -- escala / espera entre voos
"We had a four-hour layover in Madrid before the next flight."
Ground staff -- equipe de terra do aeroporto
"The ground staff at the gate helped me find a new connection."
A connecting flight -- voo de conexao
"If this one is late, I will miss my connecting flight to Rome."
To be rebooked -- ser remarcado em outro voo
"After the delay, I was rebooked onto the morning flight."
To be bumped -- ser retirado do voo por overbooking
"I was bumped from the flight, but they gave me a voucher and a hotel."
Standby -- lista de espera por um assento
"They put me on standby for the next flight, just in case."
To misplace -- extraviar / perder temporariamente
"The airline misplaced my suitcase, but it arrived a day later."
To clear security -- passar pela seguranca do aeroporto
"Once you clear security, the lounge is on your left."
The departure lounge -- sala de embarque
"We relaxed in the departure lounge until they called our gate."
Baggage claim -- esteira de retirada de bagagem
"After we landed, I waited ages at baggage claim for my bag."
Customs -- alfandega
"We had nothing to declare, so customs was quick."
Jet lag -- cansaco do fuso horario
"The jet lag was so bad that I slept all afternoon."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Overbooked
A layover
Ground staff
A connecting flight
To be rebooked
To be bumped
Standby
To misplace
To clear security
The departure lounge
Baggage claim
Customs
Jet lag

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's airport story and answer the questions.

"When my flight was overbooked, the agent told me that I had been bumped, but she said they would rebook me onto the next flight. She explained that there was a long layover in Madrid, and she told me the departure lounge was near gate twelve. At baggage claim, a member of the ground staff said the airline had misplaced my suitcase, but he promised it would arrive the following day. Honestly, the jet lag was so bad that I barely remember clearing customs."

1. What did the agent tell Pricila?
A That she had been bumped and would be rebooked.
B That the flight had extra empty seats.
C That she had to buy a new ticket.
2. In reported speech, what does "will" usually become?
A "shall".
B "would".
C It stays "will".
3. What happened to her suitcase?
A It was left at home.
B The airline had misplaced it, but it would arrive the next day.
C It was never found.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Reported Speech

Grammar

Para contar o que alguem disse, o verbo geralmente "recua" um tempo: is/are -> was/were, will -> would, have/has done -> had done. Use say (sem objeto) ou tell + pessoa. / To report what someone said, the verb usually shifts back one tense: is/are -> was/were, will -> would, have/has done -> had done. Use say (no object) or tell + person.

DirectReportedPortugues
"It is overbooked."She said it was overbooked.Ela disse que estava com overbooking.
"I will rebook you."She said she would rebook me.Ela disse que me remarcaria.
"We have misplaced it."He said they had misplaced it.Ele disse que tinham extraviado.
say vs tellShe told me... / She said (that)...tell pede objeto (told me); say nao.
Common mistakeNOT "She said me." Say "She told me" or "She said that".Nunca "say me".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"The agent told that I had been bumped."
"She said she rebook me on the next flight."
"He said the airline misplaced my suitcase."
"We had a four-hour in Madrid."
"Once you security, the lounge is on the left."
"The lag was so bad that I slept all afternoon."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's airport story in order.

?After clearing security, she waited in the departure lounge for her connecting flight.
?Finally, exhausted by jet lag, she cleared customs and went straight to bed.
?First, she checked in and heard that her flight had been overbooked.
?At baggage claim, the ground staff said the airline had misplaced her suitcase.
?Then an agent told her she had been bumped but would be rebooked.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the linking in "told me" and "had been".

The agent told me I had been bumped from the flight.
O agente me disse que eu tinha sido retirada do voo.
She said they would rebook me on the next flight.
Ela disse que me remarcariam no proximo voo.
We had a long layover before our connecting flight.
Tivemos uma longa escala antes do voo de conexao.
The jet lag was so bad that I slept all afternoon.
O jet lag foi tao forte que dormi a tarde toda.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to report to a friend what the agent said about your seat. What do you say?
A "She told me that I had been bumped from the flight."
B "She said me I am bump."
C "She will tell me tomorrow."
You want to explain a long wait between two flights. Which phrase fits?
A "We had a quick customs at baggage claim."
B "We had a four-hour layover before our connecting flight."
C "We were bumped from the departure lounge."
Your bag is not on the belt. How do you report it politely?
A "You lost my flight on purpose."
B "I think the airline has misplaced my suitcase at baggage claim."
C "I want to be bumped, please."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Tell the story of a difficult journey through an airport in 5-6 sentences. Report what the staff told you (use reported speech: "She said...", "They told me..."), and use at least three airport words (overbooked, layover, rebooked, baggage claim, jet lag). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 6

1The agent told me I had been bumped.O agente me disse que eu tinha sido retirada do voo.
2She said they would rebook me on the next flight.Ela disse que me remarcariam no proximo voo.
3We had a long layover before our connecting flight.Tivemos uma longa escala antes do voo de conexao.
4The airline had misplaced my suitcase.A companhia tinha extraviado minha mala.
5The jet lag was so bad that I slept all afternoon.O jet lag foi tao forte que dormi a tarde toda.
Aula 7 -- Pre-class

The Perfect Stay: Hotels

Resolva pedidos e reclamacoes no hotel com vocabulario B2 (upgrade, concierge, late check-out, discrepancy) e relate perguntas e ordens com reported questions & commands (I asked whether... / She told me to...).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for the Hotel

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for a smooth hotel stay.

An upgrade -- melhoria de quarto ou servico
"Because it was our anniversary, they gave us a free upgrade."
A suite -- suite / conjunto de quartos
"They upgraded us to a suite with a separate living room."
Courtesy -- cortesia / gentileza
"As a courtesy, the hotel offered us a free breakfast."
The concierge -- concierge (funcionario que organiza pedidos)
"The concierge booked us a table at the best restaurant in town."
Room service -- servico de quarto
"We were too tired to go out, so we ordered room service."
Housekeeping -- arrumacao / equipe de limpeza
"I asked housekeeping for extra towels and a spare blanket."
A wake-up call -- chamada do hotel para te acordar
"I asked the front desk for a wake-up call at six."
Late check-out -- saida tardia do quarto
"Our flight was in the evening, so we requested a late check-out."
A discrepancy -- divergencia entre valores
"There was a discrepancy on my bill, so I asked them to check it."
To escalate a complaint -- levar uma reclamacao ao gerente
"The clerk could not help, so I had to escalate the complaint."
A vacancy -- quarto livre e disponivel
"Luckily the hotel had a last-minute vacancy for the night."
Valet parking -- servico de manobrista
"There was no space outside, so we used the hotel's valet parking."
To accommodate -- acomodar / atender a um pedido
"The hotel was happy to accommodate our request for an early check-in."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

An upgrade
A suite
Courtesy
The concierge
Room service
Housekeeping
A wake-up call
Late check-out
A discrepancy
To escalate a complaint
A vacancy
Valet parking
To accommodate

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's hotel story and answer the questions.

"At the front desk, I asked whether they had any vacancies, and the receptionist told me that they did. I asked if we could have a late check-out, and she explained that housekeeping would need the room by two. She asked me what time I wanted my wake-up call, and then she told me to keep my key card away from my phone. When I noticed a discrepancy on the bill, the concierge asked me to wait a moment and told me not to worry -- as a courtesy, he removed the charge."

1. How do you report a yes/no question?
A With if/whether + statement order ("I asked whether they had a room").
B With do/did ("I asked did they have a room").
C With will ("I asked will they have a room").
2. How do you report a request or command?
A With that + past simple.
B With told/asked + person + to + verb ("She told me to wait").
C With whether + verb.
3. What did the concierge do about the discrepancy?
A He asked her to pay it twice.
B He asked her to wait and, as a courtesy, removed the charge.
C He refused to help.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Reported Questions & Commands

Grammar

Pergunta sim/nao vira asked if/whether + sujeito + verbo (ordem de afirmacao, sem do/did). Pergunta com wh- vira asked what/where/when + sujeito + verbo. Pedido ou ordem vira asked/told + pessoa + to + verbo (negativa: not to). / A yes/no question becomes asked if/whether + subject + verb (statement order, no do/did). A wh- question becomes asked what/where/when + subject + verb. A request or order becomes asked/told + person + to + verb (negative: not to).

DirectReportedPortugues
"Do you have a room?"I asked whether they had a room.Perguntei se tinham quarto.
"What time is check-out?"I asked what time check-out was.Perguntei que horas era o check-out.
"Please wait."She asked me to wait.Ela me pediu para esperar.
"Don't worry."He told me not to worry.Ele me disse para nao me preocupar.
Common mistakeNOT "I asked do they have a room." Say "I asked whether they had a room."Sem do/did; ordem de afirmacao.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"I asked they had any vacancies."
"She asked me what time I my wake-up call."
"The concierge asked me wait a moment."
"He told me to worry about the bill."
"There was a on my bill."
"As a , the hotel offered a free breakfast."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's hotel story in order.

?The concierge told her to relax and arranged a table for dinner.
?Finally, as a courtesy, the manager removed the charge and apologised.
?First, she asked whether the hotel had any vacancies for the night.
?Later, she noticed a discrepancy on the bill and decided to escalate the complaint.
?Then she requested a late check-out and an upgrade to a suite.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the stress on the reporting verbs.

I asked whether they had a late check-out available.
Perguntei se eles tinham saida tardia disponivel.
The concierge told me not to worry about the bill.
O concierge me disse para nao me preocupar com a conta.
As a courtesy, they upgraded us to a suite.
Como cortesia, nos deram um upgrade para uma suite.
I had to escalate the complaint to the manager.
Tive que levar a reclamacao ao gerente.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to report that you asked about room availability. What do you say?
A "I asked whether they had any vacancies for the weekend."
B "I asked do they have vacancies weekend."
C "I told a vacancy to the weekend."
A charge looks wrong and the clerk cannot fix it. What do you say?
A "Please give me a wake-up call about it."
B "I'd like to escalate this complaint -- there's a discrepancy on my bill."
C "I want valet parking for the discrepancy."
You would like a nicer room. How do you ask politely?
A "Give me your best suite now."
B "Would it be possible to upgrade us to a suite?"
C "I asked housekeeping to buy a suite."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe a hotel stay in 5-6 sentences. Report two things you asked at the front desk (use reported questions: "I asked whether...", "I asked what...") and one instruction the staff gave you (use "told me to..."). Use at least three hotel words (upgrade, concierge, late check-out, discrepancy, courtesy). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 7

1I asked whether they had a late check-out available.Perguntei se tinham saida tardia disponivel.
2The concierge told me not to worry about the bill.O concierge me disse para nao me preocupar com a conta.
3As a courtesy, they upgraded us to a suite.Como cortesia, nos deram um upgrade para uma suite.
4There was a discrepancy on my bill.Havia uma divergencia na minha conta.
5I had to escalate the complaint to the manager.Tive que levar a reclamacao ao gerente.
Aula 8 -- Pre-class

Navigating New Cities

Descreva transito e deslocamento com vocabulario B2 (commute, congestion, gridlock, shortcut, public transit) e conecte ideias com relative clauses (who/which/that/where, defining vs non-defining).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Getting Around

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for traffic and city travel.

Commute -- trajeto diario casa-trabalho
"My daily commute takes nearly an hour each way."
Congestion -- transito pesado e lento
"There is always heavy congestion on the bridge in the morning."
A detour -- desvio da rota direta
"The road was closed, so we had to take a long detour."
A roundabout -- rotatoria
"At the roundabout, take the third exit toward the centre."
Rush hour -- horario de pico
"We left early to avoid the rush hour traffic."
A one-way street -- rua de mao unica
"You can't turn here -- it's a one-way street."
To hail a cab -- acenar para parar um taxi
"It was raining hard, so we tried to hail a cab outside the station."
Gridlock -- engarrafamento total, nada anda
"After the accident, the whole centre was in total gridlock."
A crosswalk -- faixa de pedestres
"Always wait at the crosswalk until the light turns green."
Public transit -- transporte publico
"This city's public transit is cheap, fast and easy to use."
A shortcut -- atalho / caminho mais rapido
"The driver knew a shortcut that saved us twenty minutes."
A pedestrian -- pedestre
"Drivers must stop for any pedestrian on the crosswalk."
To pull over -- encostar o carro na lateral
"The driver pulled over to let me read the map."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Commute
Congestion
A detour
A roundabout
Rush hour
A one-way street
To hail a cab
Gridlock
A crosswalk
Public transit
A shortcut
A pedestrian
To pull over

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's city story and answer the questions.

"The city where I got lost has the worst congestion I have ever seen. I hailed a cab, which turned out to be a mistake, because we hit total gridlock at rush hour. The driver, who was very patient, knew a shortcut that avoided the one-way streets in the centre. He pulled over at a crosswalk where a group of pedestrians was waiting, and pointed to a metro station. Honestly, the public transit, which is fast and cheap, was the smartest choice all along."

1. In a defining clause about a thing, which word can replace "that"?
A which
B who
C where
2. What is true about a non-defining clause (extra information)?
A It has no commas and can use "that".
B It uses commas and who/which, never "that".
C It always starts with "where".
3. Which word introduces a clause about a place?
A who
B where
C which

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Relative Clauses

Grammar

Clausula defining da informacao essencial: sem virgulas, com who (pessoas), that/which (coisas), where (lugares). Clausula non-defining da um extra: com virgulas e who/which (NUNCA that). / A defining clause gives essential information: no commas, with who (people), that/which (things), where (places). A non-defining clause adds extra information: with commas and who/which (NEVER that).

Tipo / TypeEnglishPortugues
Defining (people)The driver who knew a shortcut.O motorista que sabia um atalho.
Defining (things)The route that avoids the centre.A rota que evita o centro.
Non-defining (commas)The taxi, which was expensive, hit gridlock.O taxi, que era caro, pegou engarrafamento.
Place (where)The corner where we pulled over.A esquina onde encostamos.
Common mistakeNOT "The taxi, that was expensive..." Use which with commas.Non-defining nao usa that.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"The driver knew a shortcut saved us time."
"We took a route avoided the one-way streets."
"He pulled over at the corner the pedestrians were waiting."
"There is always heavy on the bridge."
"We left early to avoid the hour."
"Always wait at the until the light turns green."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's city story in order.

?The driver, who was very patient, found a shortcut around the one-way streets.
?Finally, she took the public transit, which was faster and much cheaper.
?First, she got lost in a city where the congestion was terrible.
?He pulled over at a crosswalk and pointed her toward the metro.
?Then she hailed a cab, which soon got stuck in total gridlock.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice how the relative clause flows without a pause in defining clauses.

The driver who knew a shortcut saved us twenty minutes.
O motorista que sabia um atalho economizou vinte minutos.
We got stuck in total gridlock at rush hour.
Ficamos presos num engarrafamento total no horario de pico.
The public transit, which is fast and cheap, is the best choice.
O transporte publico, que e rapido e barato, e a melhor escolha.
He pulled over at the crosswalk to let me out.
Ele encostou na faixa de pedestres para me deixar sair.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You describe the driver who helped you (essential information). What do you say?
A "The driver who knew a shortcut got us there on time."
B "The driver, who knew a shortcut got us there."
C "The driver where knew a shortcut got us there."
You add extra, non-essential information about the taxi. What do you say?
A "The taxi that was quite expensive still got stuck."
B "The taxi, which was quite expensive, still got stuck in gridlock."
C "The taxi who was expensive still got stuck."
You point out a place using a relative clause. What do you say?
A "That's the corner which you can hail a cab."
B "That's the corner where you can hail a cab."
C "That's the corner who you can hail a cab."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe getting around a busy city in 5-6 sentences. Use at least two relative clauses (one with who/that and one with where) and at least three traffic words (commute, congestion, gridlock, shortcut, public transit). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 8

1The driver who knew a shortcut saved us time.O motorista que sabia um atalho economizou tempo.
2We got stuck in total gridlock at rush hour.Ficamos presos num engarrafamento total no horario de pico.
3The public transit here is fast and cheap.O transporte publico aqui e rapido e barato.
4He pulled over at the crosswalk to let me out.Ele encostou na faixa de pedestres para me deixar sair.
5This is the corner where you can hail a cab.Esta e a esquina onde da para chamar um taxi.
Aula 9 -- Pre-class

The Art of Dining Out

Descreva comida e faca pedidos com vocabulario B2 (savoury, succulent, a generous portion, to splurge on) e use o causative (have/get + objeto + participio) com so/such para enfatizar.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Dining Out

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for describing food and ordering.

Savoury -- salgado, nao doce
"I prefer something savoury, like cheese, to a sweet dessert."
Hearty -- substancioso e reconfortante
"After the hike we wanted a hearty stew."
Succulent -- suculento e macio
"The lamb was wonderfully succulent."
A generous portion -- uma porcao farta
"They gave us such a generous portion."
Peckish -- com uma fominha, nao faminto
"I am only peckish, so a starter is enough."
A craving -- um desejo forte por algo
"I have a real craving for chocolate."
To cater for -- atender a uma dieta ou necessidade
"Do you cater for gluten-free diets?"
To be in the mood for -- estar a fim de comer algo
"I am really in the mood for seafood."
To swap -- trocar um item por outro
"Could I swap the fries for a salad?"
Bland -- sem graca, sem tempero
"The soup was a bit bland, so I added salt."
To splurge on -- gastar muito num mimo
"On holiday we splurge on a fine dinner."
To whip up -- preparar um prato rapidinho
"The chef whipped up a special for us."
To box up -- embalar as sobras para levar
"Could you box up the rest, please?"

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Savoury
Hearty
Succulent
A generous portion
Peckish
A craving
To cater for
To be in the mood for
To swap
Bland
To splurge on
To whip up
To box up

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's restaurant story and answer the questions.

"When we splurge on a special dinner, I love having the meat cooked exactly how I like it. Last night the lamb was so succulent that I nearly ordered a second plate. I asked the waiter to get my fries swapped for a salad, and I had the sauce served on the side. It was such a generous portion that we could not finish it, so we had the rest boxed up to take home. The chef even whipped up a savoury little extra -- the whole meal was so good that we can't stop talking about it."

1. Which structure means you arrange for someone else to do something?
A have/get + object + past participle ("I had the steak cooked").
B will + base verb.
C going to + verb.
2. When do you use "such"?
A Before an adjective alone ("such succulent").
B Before a noun phrase ("such a generous portion").
C Only in questions.
3. What did they do with the leftovers?
A They left them on the table.
B They had them boxed up to take home.
C They gave them to the chef.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Causative + so/such

Grammar

Para dizer que voce faz alguem fazer algo por voce, use have/get + objeto + participio passado. Para enfatizar, use so + adjetivo e such + (a/an) + substantivo. / To say you arrange for someone to do something for you, use have/get + object + past participle. To emphasise, use so + adjective and such + (a/an) + noun.

Uso / UseEnglishPortugues
Causative (formal)I had the steak cooked rare.Mandei preparar o bife mal passado.
Causative (get)Could I get it changed?Da para trocar?
so + adjectiveThe lamb was so succulent.O cordeiro estava tao suculento.
such + noun phraseIt was such a generous portion.Era uma porcao tao generosa.
Common mistakeNOT "so a generous portion." Say "such a generous portion."such + substantivo; so + adjetivo.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"I had the steak rare."
"Could I my fries changed for a salad?"
"The lamb was succulent that I ordered more."
"It was a generous portion that we couldn't finish."
"Could you up the rest, please?"
"Do you for gluten-free diets?"

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's dining story in order.

?The portion was so generous that she could not finish it.
?Finally, she splurged on dessert because the meal was such a success.
?First, feeling peckish, she chose a restaurant that catered for her cravings.
?She had the leftovers boxed up and the chef whipped up a savoury treat.
?Then she had the steak cooked rare and asked to swap the fries for a salad.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the stress on the past participle in the causative.

I'll have the steak cooked rare, please.
Vou querer o bife mal passado, por favor.
The lamb was so succulent that I ordered more.
O cordeiro estava tao suculento que pedi mais.
It was such a generous portion that we boxed it up.
Era uma porcao tao generosa que embalamos.
Could I get the fries swapped for a salad?
Da para trocar a batata por salada?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want the kitchen to prepare your steak a certain way. What do you say?
A "I'll have the steak cooked medium, please."
B "I cook the steak medium for you."
C "The steak has me cooked medium."
You politely want to change a side dish. What do you say?
A "I box up the fries for a salad."
B "Could I get the fries swapped for a salad?"
C "I am so a salad, please."
You react to a huge, delicious plate. What do you say?
A "It was so a generous portion that we left."
B "It was such a generous portion that we had to box it up."
C "It was such generous that we had to box it."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe a memorable meal out in 5-6 sentences. Use at least one causative form (have/get + object + past participle) and one so/such structure, plus at least three food words (savoury, succulent, generous portion, craving, splurge). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 9

1I'll have the steak cooked rare, please.Vou querer o bife mal passado, por favor.
2The lamb was so succulent that I ordered more.O cordeiro estava tao suculento que pedi mais.
3It was such a generous portion that we boxed it up.Era uma porcao tao generosa que embalamos.
4Could I get the fries swapped for a salad?Da para trocar a batata por salada?
5Do you cater for gluten-free diets?Voces atendem dietas sem gluten?
Aula 10 -- Pre-class

Shopping & Smart Spending

Compre bem e negocie com vocabulario B2 (value for money, overpriced, to haggle, a steal, on a tight budget) e compare precos com comparatives + modifiers (far/much/slightly + comparativo, not nearly as...as).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Smart Spending

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for shopping and prices.

Value for money -- bom custo-beneficio
"At that price, the jacket was real value for money."
Overpriced -- caro demais para o que vale
"The souvenirs in the square were badly overpriced."
A rip-off -- um roubo, preco abusivo
"Twenty euros for a coffee? What a rip-off!"
Pricey -- meio caro (informal)
"That boutique is lovely, but a bit pricey."
To haggle -- pechinchar / negociar o preco
"In the market it is normal to haggle a little."
To talk someone down -- convencer a baixar o preco
"I managed to talk him down by ten euros."
A knock-off -- falsificacao barata de marca
"That 'designer' bag is a knock-off."
A steal -- uma pechincha, muito barato
"At ten euros, this jacket was a steal."
To browse -- dar uma olhada sem pressa
"I love to browse the stalls before deciding."
To shop around -- pesquisar precos em varias lojas
"It pays to shop around for the best deal."
An impulse buy -- compra por impulso
"The scarf was a total impulse buy."
Top of the range -- o melhor e mais caro da categoria
"This is the top of the range model."
On a tight budget -- com orcamento apertado
"We are on a tight budget this trip."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Value for money
Overpriced
A rip-off
Pricey
To haggle
To talk someone down
A knock-off
A steal
To browse
To shop around
An impulse buy
Top of the range
On a tight budget

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's shopping story and answer the questions.

"The market was far cheaper than the boutique in the square, which was badly overpriced. The first scarf I saw was slightly pricier than I wanted, so I decided to shop around. In the end I managed to talk the seller down, and the jacket I bought was considerably better value for money than the 'designer' knock-off next door. Honestly, the tourist shops are not nearly as good as the local stalls -- at ten euros, my jacket was an absolute steal."

1. Which modifier shows a BIG difference?
A far / considerably / much
B slightly / a bit
C very / really
2. Which modifier shows a SMALL difference?
A far / much
B slightly / a bit
C not nearly
3. What does "not nearly as good as" mean?
A Just as good as.
B Much worse than / far below.
C Slightly better than.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Comparatives with Modifiers

Grammar

Para mostrar uma diferenca grande, use far / much / considerably + comparativo. Para uma diferenca pequena, use slightly / a bit + comparativo. Para dizer "muito menos que", use not nearly as + adjetivo + as. / To show a big difference, use far / much / considerably + comparative. For a small difference, use slightly / a bit + comparative. To say "much less than", use not nearly as + adjective + as.

Ideia / IdeaEnglishPortugues
Big differenceThe market is far cheaper than the mall.O mercado e bem mais barato que o shopping.
Small differenceThis one is slightly pricier.Esse aqui e um pouco mais caro.
Much less thanThe copy is not nearly as good as the original.A copia esta longe de ser tao boa quanto a original.
EmphasisIt's considerably better value for money.Tem um custo-beneficio bem melhor.
Common mistakeNOT "far cheap." Say "far cheaper" (modifier + comparative).Modificador + forma comparativa.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"The market was cheaper than the boutique."
"This scarf is pricier than I wanted."
"The copy is not as good as the original."
"At ten euros, this jacket was a real ."
"It pays to shop for the best deal."
"The souvenirs were badly ."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's shopping story in order.

?She spotted a knock-off bag that was badly overpriced and walked away.
?Finally, she bought a jacket that was such good value for money it felt like a steal.
?First, on a tight budget, she decided to shop around before buying anything.
?At the next stall she haggled and talked the seller down by ten euros.
?Then she browsed the stalls, which were far cheaper than the tourist shops.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the stress on the modifier before the comparative.

The market was far cheaper than the boutique.
O mercado era bem mais barato que a butique.
This one is only slightly pricier than the other.
Esse aqui e so um pouco mais caro que o outro.
The copy is not nearly as good as the original.
A copia esta longe de ser tao boa quanto a original.
At ten euros, this jacket was a real steal.
A dez euros, essa jaqueta foi uma pechincha.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to say the mall is much more expensive than the market. What do you say?
A "The mall is far more expensive than the local market."
B "The mall is far expensive than the market."
C "The mall is slightly a rip-off market."
You want to negotiate a lower price politely. What do you say?
A "This is a knock-off, give it to me free."
B "It's a bit pricey -- could you come down a little?"
C "I am on a tight budget than you."
You want to describe a great bargain. What do you say?
A "It was overpriced, a real steal."
B "At that price it was real value for money -- an absolute steal."
C "It was not nearly as a steal as money."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe a shopping trip on holiday in 5-6 sentences. Compare two places or items using modifiers (far/much/slightly + comparative, or not nearly as...as), and use at least three shopping words (overpriced, haggle, value for money, a steal, on a tight budget). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 10

1The market was far cheaper than the boutique.O mercado era bem mais barato que a butique.
2This one is only slightly pricier than the other.Esse aqui e so um pouco mais caro que o outro.
3The copy is not nearly as good as the original.A copia esta longe de ser tao boa quanto a original.
4It pays to shop around for the best deal.Vale a pena pesquisar precos para achar a melhor oferta.
5At ten euros, this jacket was a real steal.A dez euros, essa jaqueta foi uma pechincha.
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

The Confident Conversationalist

Puxe conversa com naturalidade usando vocabulario B2 (strike up a conversation, hit it off, rapport, tactful, put someone at ease) e faca perguntas educadas com indirect questions (Could you tell me where...? / Do you know if...?).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Great Conversations

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for confident small talk.

To strike up a conversation -- puxar conversa
"She struck up a conversation with the guest beside her."
To hit it off -- se dar bem logo de cara
"We hit it off right away and talked for hours."
Rapport -- sintonia / boa conexao
"A good teacher builds rapport with every student."
An acquaintance -- um conhecido, nao amigo intimo
"He's an acquaintance from my old running club."
A lull -- uma pausa ou silencio na conversa
"There was an awkward lull, so I asked about her trip."
To read the room -- sentir o clima do ambiente
"He read the room and changed the subject gently."
Tactful -- com tato / diplomatico
"She gave a tactful answer that hurt no one."
To chime in -- dar um pitaco / entrar na conversa
"May I chime in? I think there's another option."
To change the subject -- mudar de assunto
"It got tense, so I gently changed the subject."
To put someone at ease -- deixar alguem a vontade
"His warm smile put me completely at ease."
To come across as -- passar a impressao de
"Asked directly, it can come across as rude."
To keep the conversation going -- manter a conversa fluindo
"A follow-up question keeps the conversation going."
To make a good impression -- causar boa impressao
"She made a good impression on her new neighbours."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

To strike up a conversation
To hit it off
Rapport
An acquaintance
A lull
To read the room
Tactful
To chime in
To change the subject
To put someone at ease
To come across as
To keep the conversation going
To make a good impression

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's networking story and answer the questions.

"At the reception, I wanted to strike up a conversation, so I asked politely, Could you tell me where the coffee is? It is much softer than a direct question, and it helps you come across as tactful. When there was an awkward lull, I said, Do you know if the tour starts at nine? We hit it off, built a real rapport, and I could tell my indirect questions had put her at ease. Honestly, knowing how to ask Would you mind telling me what time it is makes a good impression everywhere."

1. After "where" in an indirect question, what word order do we use?
A Statement order ("where the station is").
B Question order ("where is the station").
C With do/does ("where does the station is").
2. How do you make a yes/no question indirect?
A Add "that".
B Add if/whether ("Do you know if it is far?").
C Add "where".
3. Why use indirect questions?
A They are faster.
B They sound more polite and tactful.
C They are grammatically required.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Indirect Questions

Grammar

Para soar mais educado, comece com Could you tell me... / Do you know... e depois use ordem de afirmacao (sem inverter, sem do/does). Pergunta sim/nao leva if/whether. / To sound more polite, start with Could you tell me... / Do you know... and then use statement order (no inversion, no do/does). A yes/no question takes if/whether.

DirectIndirectPortugues
"Where is the station?"Could you tell me where the station is?Voce pode me dizer onde fica a estacao?
"What time does it open?"Do you know what time it opens?Voce sabe que horas abre?
"Is it far?"Do you know if it is far?Voce sabe se e longe?
