PolitenessWould you mind telling me...?Voce se importaria de me dizer...?
Common mistakeNOT "Could you tell me where is the station?" Use "where the station is."Ordem de afirmacao, sem inverter.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"Could you tell me where the station ?"
"Do you know the tour starts at nine?"
"Would you telling me what time it is?"
"There was an awkward , so I asked about her trip."
"We hit it and talked for hours."
"A follow-up question keeps the conversation ."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's networking story in order.

?During an awkward lull, she read the room and gently changed the subject.
?Finally, her indirect questions had put everyone at ease, and the evening flew by.
?First, wanting to make a good impression, she struck up a conversation at the reception.
?They quickly hit it off and built a warm rapport.
?She asked where the tour met, which sounded tactful and polite.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the gentle, rising tone of a polite question.

Could you tell me where the station is?
Voce pode me dizer onde fica a estacao?
Do you know if the tour starts at nine?
Voce sabe se o passeio comeca as nove?
A tactful question really helps you make a good impression.
Uma pergunta com tato ajuda muito a causar boa impressao.
We hit it off and built a real rapport.
A gente se deu bem e criou uma verdadeira sintonia.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to ask for directions politely. What do you say?
A "Could you tell me where the nearest station is?"
B "Could you tell me where is the nearest station?"
C "Where the station, please tell?"
You want to check a detail politely (yes/no). What do you say?
A "Do you know is breakfast included?"
B "Do you know if breakfast is included?"
C "Breakfast is included, do you know?"
There is an awkward silence. What is a smooth thing to do?
A "I come across as a lull to the rapport."
B "During the lull, I read the room and changed the subject."
C "I put the subject at ease with a stranger."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe meeting someone new at an event in 5-6 sentences. Use at least two indirect questions (one with where/what and one with if/whether) and at least three conversation words (strike up a conversation, hit it off, rapport, tactful, put someone at ease). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 11

1Could you tell me where the station is?Voce pode me dizer onde fica a estacao?
2Do you know if the tour starts at nine?Voce sabe se o passeio comeca as nove?
3We hit it off and built a real rapport.A gente se deu bem e criou uma verdadeira sintonia.
4A tactful question makes a good impression.Uma pergunta com tato causa boa impressao.
5Her warm smile put me completely at ease.O sorriso caloroso dela me deixou totalmente a vontade.
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

Diplomatic Disagreement

Discorde com elegancia usando vocabulario B2 (beg to differ, a fair point, common ground, meet halfway, agree to disagree) e conecte ideias opostas com conjuncoes de contraste (although / whereas / even though / while).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Polite Disagreement

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for disagreeing without conflict.

To beg to differ -- discordar educadamente
"I'm afraid I beg to differ -- the coast is the best part."
A fair point -- um argumento valido e razoavel
"That's a fair point -- the queues really are long."
To push back -- resistir ou questionar com jeito
"She didn't argue, but she pushed back on the price."
Common ground -- um ponto em comum
"We found common ground: we both love good food."
To concede -- admitir / ceder um ponto
"I'll concede the city is crowded in summer."
To be at odds -- estar em desacordo
"We were at odds about the route, but stayed friendly."
To meet halfway -- chegar a um meio-termo
"We met halfway: a morning in the museum, an afternoon by the sea."
To agree to disagree -- aceitar que discordam e seguir
"In the end, we just agreed to disagree about the hotel."
A counterargument -- um contra-argumento
"Her counterargument was that sunsets there are unforgettable."
With all due respect -- com todo o respeito (antes de discordar)
"With all due respect, I think the coast is worth the time."
To take issue with -- discordar de / objetar a algo
"I take issue with the idea that markets are a waste of time."
To play devil's advocate -- bancar o advogado do diabo
"Let me play devil's advocate: what if we stayed one more day?"
With respect, I see it differently -- com respeito, vejo de outra forma
"With respect, I see it differently -- the trip was worth it."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

To beg to differ
A fair point
To push back
Common ground
To concede
To be at odds
To meet halfway
To agree to disagree
A counterargument
With all due respect
To take issue with
To play devil's advocate
With respect, I see it differently

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's story about a friendly disagreement and answer the questions.

"My friend loves cities, whereas I much prefer the coast. Although we were at odds about the trip, we stayed friendly and looked for common ground. Even though she made a fair point about the museums, I begged to differ about spending three whole days there. While she pushed back gently, I conceded that the old town was beautiful. In the end, although neither of us fully agreed, we met halfway and agreed to disagree about the rest."

1. Do although / whereas / even though / while join two full clauses?
A Yes, each with its own subject and verb.
B No, they join two nouns.
C They can only start a question.
2. Do these words signal agreement or contrast?
A Agreement.
B Contrast.
C Cause and effect.
3. How did they resolve the disagreement?
A One of them cancelled the trip.
B They met halfway and agreed to disagree.
C They stopped speaking to each other.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Contrast Conjunctions

Grammar

Although, even though, whereas e while ligam duas oracoes completas (cada uma com sujeito e verbo) para mostrar CONTRASTE. Nao use but junto de although na mesma frase. / Although, even though, whereas and while join two full clauses (each with a subject and verb) to show CONTRAST. Do not use but together with although in the same sentence.

Conjuncao / WordEnglishPortugues
althoughAlthough we disagreed, we stayed friends.Embora discordassemos, continuamos amigos.
even though (stronger)Even though she made a fair point, I disagreed.Mesmo ela tendo razao, discordei.
whereas (comparing)She loves cities, whereas I prefer the coast.Ela ama cidades, ao passo que prefiro o litoral.
while (contrast)While she pushed back, I conceded.Enquanto ela resistia, eu cedia.
Common mistakeNOT "Although we disagreed, but we stayed friends." Drop "but".Nao use but com although.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

" we were at odds, we stayed friendly."
"She loves cities, I prefer the coast."
" though she made a fair point, I disagreed."
"I'll that the city is crowded in summer."
"With all due , I see it differently."
"We found common : we both love food."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's disagreement story in order.

?While her friend pushed back, Pricila conceded that the old town was lovely.
?In the end, with all due respect, they simply agreed to disagree.
?First, they realised they were at odds about the whole itinerary.
?They looked for common ground and finally met halfway.
?Although she made a fair point about the museums, Pricila begged to differ.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the calm, respectful tone.

Although we were at odds, we stayed friendly.
Embora estivessemos em desacordo, continuamos amigaveis.
With all due respect, I see it differently.
Com todo o respeito, eu vejo diferente.
You make a fair point, but I beg to differ.
Voce faz um bom argumento, mas eu discordo.
In the end, we agreed to disagree.
No fim, aceitamos que discordamos.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to disagree with a colleague politely. What do you say?
A "With all due respect, I see it differently."
B "You are completely wrong."
C "That is a stupid idea."
You want to accept a valid point before disagreeing. What do you say?
A "That makes no sense at all."
B "You make a fair point, but I still beg to differ."
C "I take issue with everything you say."
You want to describe reaching a compromise. What do you say?
A "We were at odds, so we cancelled everything."
B "We were at odds, but we met halfway and found common ground."
C "We agreed to disagree by shouting louder."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Describe a friendly disagreement you have had in 5-6 sentences. Use at least two contrast conjunctions (although, even though, whereas, while) and at least three phrases from this lesson (beg to differ, a fair point, common ground, meet halfway, agree to disagree). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 12

1Although we were at odds, we stayed friendly.Embora estivessemos em desacordo, continuamos amigaveis.
2With all due respect, I see it differently.Com todo o respeito, eu vejo diferente.
3You make a fair point, but I beg to differ.Voce faz um bom argumento, mas eu discordo.
4Let's meet halfway and find common ground.Vamos chegar a um meio-termo e achar um ponto em comum.
5In the end, we agreed to disagree.No fim, aceitamos que discordamos.
Aula 13 -- Pre-class

Stories Worth Telling

Conte historias de viagem com vocabulario B2 (anecdote, eventful, run into, get lost, mishap, end up) e domine a narrativa no passado: past continuous (fundo) vs past simple (evento) + used to (habito antigo).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Telling Stories

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for telling a memorable travel story.

An anecdote -- uma historinha pessoal
"She told a funny anecdote about getting lost in Rome."
Vivid -- nitido e forte na memoria
"I still have a vivid memory of that evening."
To recall -- relembrar / trazer a memoria
"I can still recall the smell of the market."
Eventful -- cheio de acontecimentos
"It was the most eventful day of the whole trip."
Unexpected -- inesperado, sem planejar
"Then something completely unexpected happened."
A turning point -- um momento que muda tudo
"That trip was a real turning point for me."
To set off -- partir / comecar a viagem
"We set off early, before sunrise."
To run into -- encontrar alguem por acaso
"I ran into an old friend at the airport."
To get lost -- se perder no caminho
"We got lost in the old town for an hour."
A mishap -- um contratempo / pequeno acidente
"Despite a few mishaps, the trip was wonderful."
To end up -- acabar em um lugar ou situacao
"We ended up in a tiny village we had never planned to visit."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

An anecdote
Vivid
To recall
Eventful
Unexpected
A turning point
To set off
To run into
To get lost
A mishap
To end up

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's travel anecdote and answer the questions.

"I still recall one especially eventful day. We had set off early, and we used to travel light, so we only had a small bag each. While we were wandering through the old town, we got lost, and then something completely unexpected happened: I ran into an old friend I had not seen in years. Despite a few mishaps, we ended up in a tiny village we had never planned to visit. It became a real turning point, and I still have a vivid memory of it."

1. In "I was walking when it rained", which verb is the background action?
A was walking (past continuous)
B rained (past simple)
C Neither of them
2. After "used to", do we use the base verb or the -ing form?
A The base verb ("used to travel")
B The -ing form ("used to travelling")
C The past form ("used to travelled")
3. Which tense marks the sudden, interrupting event?
A Past continuous
B Past simple ("I ran into...")
C Present perfect

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Past Narrative (Continuous vs Simple, used to)

Grammar

Use o past continuous (was/were + -ing) para o FUNDO da cena e o past simple para o EVENTO que interrompe. Para um habito do passado, use used to + verbo base. / Use the past continuous (was/were + -ing) for the BACKGROUND of the scene and the past simple for the EVENT that interrupts. For a past habit, use used to + base verb.

Uso / UseEnglishPortugues
BackgroundWhile we were wandering, we got lost.Enquanto andavamos, nos perdemos.
Interrupting eventI ran into an old friend.Encontrei um velho amigo por acaso.
Past habit (used to + base)We used to travel light.Costumavamos viajar leve.
SequenceWe set off, got lost, and ended up in a village.Partimos, nos perdemos e acabamos numa vila.
Common mistakeNOT "used to travelling." Say "used to travel" (base verb).used to + verbo base.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"While we wandering, we got lost."
"We used to light on every trip."
"Then I into an old friend at the airport."
"It was the most day of the whole trip."
"I still have a memory of that evening."
"We up in a village we never planned to visit."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's travel anecdote in order.

?Then something unexpected happened: I ran into a long-lost friend.
?Looking back, it was a real turning point and a vivid, unforgettable memory.
?First, we set off early, before sunrise.
?After a few mishaps, we ended up in a tiny village.
?While we were exploring the old town, we got completely lost.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the flow between the background and the event.

While we were wandering, we got lost.
Enquanto andavamos, nos perdemos.
We used to travel light on every trip.
Costumavamos viajar leve em toda viagem.
Then I ran into an old friend at the airport.
Ai encontrei por acaso um velho amigo no aeroporto.
I still have a vivid memory of that day.
Ainda tenho uma lembranca nitida daquele dia.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to set the background of a story. What do you say?
A "I was walking through the market when it suddenly started to rain."
B "I walk through the market when it rains."
C "I have walked through the market."
You want to talk about a past habit. What do you say?
A "We use to setting off before sunrise."
B "We used to set off before sunrise on every trip."
C "We are used to set off before sunrise."
You want to describe a surprising twist. What do you say?
A "Nothing happened, as we planned."
B "Then something unexpected happened and we ended up in a tiny village."
C "We used to ending up in villages."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Tell a short travel story in 5-6 sentences. Use the past continuous for the background (I was ...ing when...), the past simple for the events, and "used to" for a past habit. Include at least three words from this lesson (anecdote, eventful, run into, get lost, mishap, end up). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 13

1While we were wandering, we got lost.Enquanto andavamos, nos perdemos.
2We used to travel light on every trip.Costumavamos viajar leve em toda viagem.
3Then I ran into an old friend at the airport.Ai encontrei por acaso um velho amigo no aeroporto.
4After a few mishaps, we ended up in a village.Depois de alguns contratempos, acabamos numa vila.
5I still have a vivid memory of that day.Ainda tenho uma lembranca nitida daquele dia.
Aula 14 -- Pre-class

Staying in Touch

Mantenha laços à distância com vocabulario B2 (keep in touch, reach out, catch up, keep someone posted, look someone up) e fale de planos com future time clauses (as soon as / when / once + presente, will no resultado).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Staying Connected

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for keeping in contact.

To keep in touch -- manter contato
"We promised to keep in touch after the trip."
To lose touch -- perder o contato
"We slowly lost touch after she moved away."
To drop someone a line -- mandar um recado rapido
"Drop me a line when you land safely."
To catch up -- colocar o papo em dia
"Let's catch up over coffee when I'm back."
To reach out -- tomar a iniciativa de contatar
"I'll reach out as soon as things calm down."
To fall out of touch -- ir perdendo o contato aos poucos
"After a busy year, we fell out of touch."
To touch base -- dar um alo rapido para se atualizar
"Let's touch base next week about the trip."
To reconnect -- reconectar / retomar o contato
"It was lovely to reconnect after all these years."
A catch-up -- uma conversa para por o papo em dia
"We had a long catch-up over dinner."
To keep someone posted -- manter alguem informado
"Once you book anything, keep me posted."
To check in on -- ver como alguem esta
"I message her every few weeks to check in on her."
A long-lost friend -- um amigo que sumiu ha muito tempo
"On the trip I ran into a long-lost friend from school."
To drift apart -- se afastar aos poucos
"We never argued; we just drifted apart."
To look someone up -- procurar e retomar contato
"If you're ever in Brazil, look me up."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

To keep in touch
To lose touch
To drop someone a line
To catch up
To reach out
To fall out of touch
To touch base
To reconnect
A catch-up
To keep someone posted
To check in on
A long-lost friend
To drift apart
To look someone up

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's message about staying in touch and answer the questions.

"When I get home, I will finally reconnect with everyone I met. As soon as I land, I will drop my sister a line so she knows I am safe. I promise I will keep you all posted the moment anything changes. Once things calm down, I will reach out and we can have a proper catch-up. And before I forget, if you are ever in Brazil, look me up -- I really do not want us to drift apart or lose touch again."

1. In "As soon as I land, I will text you", is "land" present or future in form?
A Present in form (future in meaning).
B Future ("will land").
C Past ("landed").
2. Where does "will" go -- the time clause or the result clause?
A In the time clause (after "as soon as").
B In the result clause ("I will text you").
C In both clauses.
3. After time words (when, as soon as, once), which tense do we use?
A Present simple.
B Future with will.
C Present perfect.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Future Time Clauses

Grammar

Depois de when, as soon as, once, after, before e until, use o PRESENTE simples, mesmo com sentido de futuro. O will (ou going to) fica na oracao de RESULTADO, nunca na oracao de tempo. / After when, as soon as, once, after, before and until, use the PRESENT simple, even for future meaning. Will (or going to) goes in the RESULT clause, never in the time clause.

Parte / PartEnglishPortugues
Time clause (present)As soon as I land, ...Assim que eu pousar, ...
Result clause (will)... I will text you.... eu te mando mensagem.
when + presentWhen I get home, I will call you.Quando eu chegar em casa, eu te ligo.
once + presentOnce things calm down, I will reach out.Assim que acalmar, eu procuro voce.
Common mistakeNOT "As soon as I will land." Say "As soon as I land."Presente na oracao de tempo.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"As soon as I , I will text you."
"When I get home, I reconnect with everyone."
"Once things calm down, I will out."
"Let's up over coffee when I'm back."
"Once you book anything, keep me ."
"If you're ever in Brazil, look me ."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's plan to stay in touch in order.

?When things calm down, she will reach out for a proper catch-up.
?Finally, they agreed to look each other up so they never drift apart again.
?First, before they said goodbye, they promised to keep in touch.
?She will keep everyone posted the moment her plans change.
?As soon as she lands, she will drop her sister a line.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the natural "will" contraction in speech.

As soon as I land, I will text you.
Assim que eu pousar, eu te mando mensagem.
When things calm down, I will reach out.
Quando as coisas acalmarem, eu vou procurar voce.
Let us catch up over coffee next week.
Vamos por o papo em dia num cafe semana que vem.
I really do not want us to lose touch again.
Eu nao quero mesmo que a gente perca o contato de novo.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to reassure a friend you will message on arrival. What do you say?
A "As soon as I land, I will drop you a line."
B "As soon as I will land, I drop you a line."
C "When I landed, I will text."
You want to suggest reconnecting later. What do you say?
A "Let's drift apart when I am back."
B "Once things calm down, let's catch up properly."
C "I will lose touch as soon as I can."
You want to keep a friend updated. What do you say?
A "I will keep you posted after I forgot everything."
B "I will keep you posted the moment anything changes."
C "Keep me posted when I will change my plans."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Talk about staying in touch with people after a trip in 5-6 sentences. Use at least two future time clauses (as soon as / when / once + present, will in the result) and at least three expressions from this lesson (keep in touch, reach out, catch up, keep someone posted, look someone up). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 14

1As soon as I land, I will text you.Assim que eu pousar, eu te mando mensagem.
2When things calm down, I will reach out.Quando as coisas acalmarem, eu vou procurar voce.
3Let us catch up over coffee next week.Vamos por o papo em dia num cafe semana que vem.
4I will keep you posted the moment anything changes.Vou te manter informado assim que algo mudar.
5Let us look each other up and not drift apart.Vamos nos procurar e nao nos afastar.
Aula 15 -- Pre-class

Handling the Unexpected

Lide com imprevistos de viagem usando vocabulario B2 (go pear-shaped, keep your cool, think on your feet, backup plan, roll with the punches) e o second conditional (If + past simple, would + verbo) para situacoes hipoteticas.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for the Unexpected

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for staying calm when plans go wrong.

To go pear-shaped -- dar tudo errado
"The trip started well, but then it all went pear-shaped."
A hiccup -- um pequeno contratempo
"There was a small hiccup with our booking."
To keep your cool -- manter a calma
"She kept her cool when the flight was cancelled."
A curveball -- uma surpresa dificil
"The weather threw us a real curveball."
To think on your feet -- pensar rapido / improvisar
"As a dentist, I learned to think on my feet."
A contingency plan -- um plano de contingencia
"Always have a contingency plan for delays."
A backup plan -- um plano B
"If the tour is full, we have a backup plan."
To fall through -- nao dar certo / furar
"Our hotel booking fell through at the last minute."
To make do -- se virar com o que tem
"We had to make do with a smaller room."
To roll with the punches -- se adaptar aos imprevistos
"When you travel, you learn to roll with the punches."
To take it in your stride -- encarar com naturalidade
"She took the delay in her stride."
To weather the storm -- superar um periodo dificil
"Together we weathered the storm and enjoyed the trip."
To be caught off guard -- ser pego de surpresa
"We were caught off guard by the sudden strike."
A worst-case scenario -- o pior cenario possivel
"In the worst-case scenario, we would take a taxi."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

To go pear-shaped
A hiccup
To keep your cool
A curveball
To think on your feet
A contingency plan
A backup plan
To fall through
To make do
To roll with the punches
To take it in your stride
To weather the storm
To be caught off guard
A worst-case scenario

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's advice about handling problems and answer the questions.

"I always tell people: if something went wrong on a trip, I would simply keep my cool. If I lost my passport, I would go straight to the embassy. If our hotel fell through, we would make do with somewhere simpler. Honestly, if the whole day went pear-shaped, I would just roll with the punches and take it in my stride. If I did not have a backup plan, I would feel far more anxious -- so I always prepare one."

1. In "If I lost my passport, I would go to the embassy", is the situation real or imaginary?
A A real thing that is happening now.
B Imaginary / hypothetical.
C Something that always happens.
2. Which verb form comes after "if"?
A Past simple ("lost").
B would + verb ("would lose").
C Present continuous ("losing").
3. Which form comes in the result clause?
A Past simple ("went").
B would + base verb ("would go").
C will + base verb ("will go").

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Second Conditional

Grammar

Use o second conditional para situacoes HIPOTETICAS ou improvaveis: If + past simple na condicao, would + verbo base no resultado. Depois de if NUNCA use would. / Use the second conditional for HYPOTHETICAL or unlikely situations: If + past simple in the condition, would + base verb in the result. Never use would after if.

Parte / PartEnglishPortugues
If + past simple (condition)If I lost my passport, ...Se eu perdesse meu passaporte, ...
would + base (result)... I would go to the embassy.... eu iria a embaixada.
Imaginary / unlikelyIf the plan fell through, we would make do.Se o plano furasse, a gente se viraria.
Advice (If I were you)If I were you, I would keep my cool.Se eu fosse voce, manteria a calma.
Common mistakeNOT "If I would lose my passport." Say "If I lost..."Nunca use would depois de if.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"If I my passport, I would go to the embassy."
"If the plan fell through, we make do."
"If the whole day went , I would stay calm."
"She always keeps her under pressure."
"Always have a plan for delays."
"We were caught off by the sudden strike."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's unexpected day in order.

?Thinking on her feet, she used her backup plan.
?In the end, she took the whole thing in her stride and weathered the storm.
?First, their hotel booking fell through at the very last minute.
?They had to make do with a smaller room for one night.
?Although it was a real curveball, Pricila kept her cool.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the calm, steady tone.

If I lost my passport, I would go to the embassy.
Se eu perdesse o passaporte, iria a embaixada.
If the plan fell through, we would make do.
Se o plano furasse, a gente se viraria.
She kept her cool and thought on her feet.
Ela manteve a calma e pensou rapido.
When you travel, you learn to roll with the punches.
Quando voce viaja, aprende a se adaptar aos imprevistos.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to react calmly to a hypothetical cancelled flight. What do you say?
A "If my flight were cancelled, I would stay calm and find the next one."
B "If my flight would be cancelled, I stay calm."
C "If my flight is cancelled yesterday, I panicked."
You want to describe having a plan B. What do you say?
A "If the tour fell through, I will do nothing."
B "If the tour fell through, we would use our backup plan."
C "If the tour would fall through, we make do."
You want to talk about adapting to problems. What do you say?
A "When things go pear-shaped, I give up immediately."
B "When things go pear-shaped, I just roll with the punches."
C "When things go pear-shaped, I lose my cool completely."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine something goes wrong on a trip (a lost bag, a cancelled flight, a hotel that falls through). Explain in 5-6 sentences what you would do, using the second conditional (If + past simple, would + base) at least twice and at least three expressions from this lesson (keep your cool, think on your feet, backup plan, make do, roll with the punches). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 15

1If I lost my passport, I would go to the embassy.Se eu perdesse o passaporte, iria a embaixada.
2If the plan fell through, we would make do.Se o plano furasse, a gente se viraria.
3She kept her cool and thought on her feet.Ela manteve a calma e pensou rapido.
4Always have a backup plan for delays.Sempre tenha um plano B para atrasos.
5When you travel, you learn to roll with the punches.Quando voce viaja, aprende a se adaptar.
Aula 16 -- Pre-class

Tipping & Money Matters

Entenda taxas, gorjetas e contas em viagem com vocabulario B2 (service charge, surcharge, tip, split the bill, go Dutch, keep the change) e a voz passiva (a service charge is added; the meal was paid for by my friend).
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Words for Money & Tipping

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These are rich B2 phrases for handling bills and tips.

A service charge -- uma taxa de servico
"A ten percent service charge is added to the bill."
A surcharge -- uma sobretaxa
"A small surcharge is charged for paying by card."
A markup -- uma margem / sobrepreco
"There is a big markup on drinks at the hotel."
A hidden cost -- um custo oculto
"Watch out for hidden costs when you book online."
To tip -- dar gorjeta
"In the US, you are expected to tip your waiter."
Gratuity -- gorjeta (termo formal)
"A gratuity is included for large groups."
To split the bill -- dividir a conta
"Shall we split the bill evenly?"
To go Dutch -- cada um paga o seu
"We decided to go Dutch at the restaurant."
A flat rate -- uma taxa fixa
"The taxi to the airport is a flat rate."
Loose change -- trocado / moedas soltas
"I keep some loose change for tips."
To keep the change -- ficar com o troco
"Keep the change, please."
Petty cash -- dinheiro para pequenas despesas
"We use petty cash for small tips and snacks."
A daily allowance -- uma diaria / verba diaria
"The tour gives you a daily allowance for meals."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

A service charge
A surcharge
A markup
A hidden cost
To tip
Gratuity
To split the bill
To go Dutch
A flat rate
Loose change
To keep the change
Petty cash
A daily allowance

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read Pricila's notes about travel money and answer the questions.

"When you travel, it helps to understand how prices are calculated. In many restaurants, a service charge is automatically added to the bill. A small surcharge is charged for card payments, and hidden costs are sometimes buried in the fine print. In tourist areas, drinks are usually marked up, and gratuities are expected in some countries. On my last trip, the taxi fare was set at a flat rate, and the whole meal was paid for by my friend as a treat."

1. In "A service charge is added to the bill", do we know who adds it?
A No, and it is not important (agentless passive).
B Yes, the customer adds it.
C Yes, it says the waiter's name.
2. How do we form the present passive?
A is/are + past participle ("is added").
B is/are + base verb ("is add").
C will + verb ("will add").
3. When do we use "by + agent"?
A Always, in every passive sentence.
B When the person who did it is new or important info.
C Only in questions.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Passive Voice

Grammar

Use a voz passiva (be + participio passado) quando a ACAO importa mais do que quem a fez. Se quem fez for desconhecido ou obvio, omita o agente. Use by + agente so quando for informacao nova e importante. / Use the passive (be + past participle) when the ACTION matters more than who did it. If the doer is unknown or obvious, leave the agent out. Use by + agent only when it is new, important information.

Uso / UseEnglishPortugues
Present passiveA service charge is added.Uma taxa de servico e adicionada.
Agentless (unknown)Drinks are marked up.As bebidas tem sobrepreco.
Past passiveThe fare was set at a flat rate.A tarifa foi fixada.
by + agent (new info)The meal was paid for by my friend.A refeicao foi paga pelo meu amigo.
Common mistakeNOT "A service charge is add." Say "is added" (past participle).Use o participio passado.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"A service charge is to the bill."
"A small surcharge is for card payments."
"In tourist areas, drinks are usually up."
"The taxi fare was at a flat rate."
"The whole meal was paid for my friend."
"You can just say: keep the , please."

Stage 2: Order the Story

Order

Listen first, then put Pricila's restaurant bill story in order.

?We also realised the drinks had been marked up quite a lot.
?In the end, we left some loose change and told the waiter to keep the change.
?First, we noticed that a service charge was already added to the bill.
?To keep things fair, we decided to split the bill and go Dutch.
?Then a small surcharge was charged for paying by card.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Notice the linking in the passive forms.

A service charge is added to the bill.
Uma taxa de servico e adicionada a conta.
A surcharge is charged for card payments.
Uma sobretaxa e cobrada em pagamentos com cartao.
Shall we split the bill and go Dutch?
Vamos dividir a conta, cada um paga o seu?
Keep the change, please.
Pode ficar com o troco, por favor.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to explain an added fee at a restaurant. What do you say?
A "A ten percent service charge is added to every bill here."
B "A ten percent service charge is add to every bill."
C "They is adding ten percent service charge."
You want to suggest sharing the cost fairly. What do you say?
A "I will pay for everyone and never mention it."
B "Shall we split the bill and go Dutch?"
C "Let's mark up the drinks ourselves."
You want to leave a tip informally to a taxi driver. What do you say?
A "Give me a hidden cost, please."
B "Keep the change, please."
C "A surcharge is charged to you."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Talk about how you handle money and tipping when you travel, in 5-6 sentences. Use the passive voice at least twice (a service charge is added, drinks are marked up, the fare was set) and at least three expressions from this lesson (service charge, tip, split the bill, go Dutch, keep the change, flat rate). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 16

1A service charge is added to the bill.Uma taxa de servico e adicionada a conta.
2A surcharge is charged for card payments.Uma sobretaxa e cobrada em pagamentos com cartao.
3Shall we split the bill and go Dutch?Vamos dividir a conta, cada um paga o seu?
4Keep the change, please.Pode ficar com o troco, por favor.
5The taxi to the airport is a flat rate.O taxi para o aeroporto e uma taxa fixa.
Aula 17 -- Pre-class

Looking Back, Moving Forward

Olhe para tras sem arrependimento: fale do que voce teria feito diferente com o terceiro condicional (if I had known, I would have...) e expresse desejos sobre o passado com "I wish" e "if only".
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Reflection Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These words help you look back on your life and travels.

In hindsight -- em retrospecto / olhando para tras
"In hindsight, I should have stayed a few more days."
A turning point -- um ponto de virada
"That trip was a real turning point for me."
To dwell on -- remoer / ficar pensando demais em
"Try not to dwell on the trips you missed."
No regrets -- sem arrependimentos
"I live by no regrets -- every choice brought me here."
To make the most of -- aproveitar ao maximo
"Let's make the most of the time we have left."
A leap of faith -- um salto de fe / uma aposta corajosa
"Moving abroad was a leap of faith, but I'm glad I jumped."
To come full circle -- fechar o ciclo / voltar a origem
"After all these years, my life has come full circle."
To take stock -- fazer um balanco / refletir sobre a situacao
"Let's take stock of how far we've come."
A wake-up call -- um alerta / um sinal de que e preciso mudar
"Getting lost alone was a real wake-up call."
Food for thought -- algo que da o que pensar
"His advice gave me a lot of food for thought."
To look back on -- relembrar / refletir sobre o passado
"I love to look back on that summer in Italy."
Worthwhile -- que vale a pena
"The long trip was tiring but completely worthwhile."
To second-guess -- duvidar / questionar uma decisao depois
"I try not to second-guess the choices I made."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

In hindsight
A turning point
To dwell on
No regrets
To make the most of
A leap of faith
To come full circle
To take stock
A wake-up call
Food for thought
To look back on
Worthwhile
To second-guess

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the reflection and answer the questions.

Pricila looks back on her trip to Italy. "If I had booked the full month, I would have learned the language," she thinks. "I wish I had been a little braver back then." But she smiles. Meeting an old friend on the train was a turning point. "If I hadn't taken that imperfect trip, I wouldn't have found the life I love now." In hindsight, she has no regrets at all.

1. What would Pricila have learned if she had booked the full month?
A The language.
B How to cook only.
C Nothing new.
2. What does "I wish I had been braver" express?
A A future plan.
B A regret about the past.
C A present habit.
3. How does Pricila feel about the trip in the end?
A Very regretful.
B Angry with herself.
C At peace -- no regrets.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Third Conditional & "wish"

Grammar

O terceiro condicional fala de um passado IMAGINARIO (que nao aconteceu): if + had + particio passado, would have + particio passado. "If I had known, I would have booked." NUNCA use "would" depois de "if" (NAO "if I would have known"). Para arrependimentos, use "I wish I had..." ou "If only I had...". / The third conditional talks about an unreal past: if + had + past participle, would have + past participle. Never use "would" in the if-clause. For regrets, use "I wish I had..." or "If only I had...".

Funcao / FunctionEnglishPortugues
Unreal past resultIf I had known, I would have booked earlier.Se eu soubesse, eu teria reservado antes.
Possible past resultIf we had saved, we could have gone to Japan.Se tivessemos economizado, poderiamos ter ido ao Japao.
Past regret (wish)I wish I had learned English sooner.Eu queria ter aprendido ingles mais cedo.
Strong regret (if only)If only I had taken that trip.Se eu ao menos tivesse feito aquela viagem.
Common mistakeNOT "If I would have known" / "would of"Nunca "would" no "if"; e "would have", nao "would of".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"If I had known, I would booked earlier."
"I wish I learned English sooner."
"If only I taken that trip."
"If we had saved, we could gone to Japan."
"If I made that mistake, I wouldn't have arrived here."
"In , it was a real turning point."

Stage 2: Order the Reflection

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of Pricila's reflection in order.

?In hindsight, she wishes she had stayed the full month.
?So she lets it go with no regrets and looks forward.
?First, Pricila looks back on her short trip to Italy.
?After that, she realizes it was a turning point that shaped her life.
?Then she thinks: if she had stayed, she would have learned the language.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Keep a calm, reflective voice.

If I had known, I would have booked earlier.
Se eu soubesse, eu teria reservado antes.
I wish I had learned English sooner.
Eu queria ter aprendido ingles mais cedo.
In hindsight, it was a real turning point.
Em retrospecto, foi um verdadeiro ponto de virada.
Let's make the most of the road ahead.
Vamos aproveitar ao maximo o caminho a frente.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to say you regret not booking early. What is correct?
A "If I would have booked early, I would saved money."
B "If I had booked early, I would have saved money."
C "If I book early, I save money."
You wish you had started English earlier. How do you say it?
A "I wish I had started English earlier."
B "I wish I have started English earlier."
C "I wish I would have start English earlier."
A friend keeps remembering a missed trip sadly. What do you advise?
A "You must dwell on it forever."
B "Try not to dwell on it -- no regrets, just make the most of the next one."
C "Second-guess every choice you ever made."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Look back on one trip or choice in your life. Tell us one thing you would have done differently (third conditional), one thing you wish you had done ("I wish I had..."), and the turning point it became. Finish with how you feel now -- with no regrets. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 17

1If I had known, I would have booked earlier.Se eu soubesse, eu teria reservado antes.
2I wish I had learned English sooner.Eu queria ter aprendido ingles mais cedo.
3If only I had taken that trip.Se eu ao menos tivesse feito aquela viagem.
4In hindsight, it was a real turning point.Em retrospecto, foi um verdadeiro ponto de virada.
5Let's make the most of the road ahead.Vamos aproveitar ao maximo o caminho a frente.
Aula 18 -- Pre-class

Reading People & Situations

Entenda momentos confusos no exterior e deduza o que os outros quiseram dizer no passado com must have / might have / can't have (She must have misunderstood me). Leia as pessoas com generosidade.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Reading People Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These words help you make sense of confusing social moments abroad.

To misread a situation -- interpretar mal uma situacao
"I completely misread the situation -- he wasn't angry at all."
A faux pas -- uma gafe social
"Using first names too soon can be a faux pas in some countries."
To come across as -- passar a impressao de / parecer
"I didn't mean to, but I came across as cold."
To take offence -- ofender-se / sentir-se ofendido
"Please don't take offence -- it's just a local custom."
To mean no harm -- nao ter ma intencao
"He meant no harm -- it was just a misunderstanding."
A culture clash -- um choque cultural
"It was a small culture clash, nothing more."
To give the benefit of the doubt -- dar o beneficio da duvida
"I gave her the benefit of the doubt -- she was probably just shy."
To jump to conclusions -- tirar conclusoes precipitadas
"Don't jump to conclusions -- ask her what she meant first."
Body language -- linguagem corporal
"His body language told me he was uncomfortable."
To lose face -- perder a reputacao / passar vergonha em publico
"He didn't want to lose face in front of his colleagues."
An awkward silence -- um silencio constrangedor
"There was an awkward silence after my comment."
To smooth things over -- acalmar / contornar uma situacao tensa
"A smile and an apology smoothed things over."
To read between the lines -- entender as entrelinhas
"Read between the lines -- she might just be tired."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

To misread a situation
A faux pas
To come across as
To take offence
To mean no harm
A culture clash
To give the benefit of the doubt
To jump to conclusions
Body language
To lose face
An awkward silence
To smooth things over
To read between the lines

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story and answer the questions.

In Japan, Pricila handed her business card with one hand and the man went quiet. "I must have done something wrong," she thought. But a friend explained the custom. "You can't have known the rule -- it's tiny," he said. "He might have just been surprised." Pricila realized she had jumped to conclusions. He can't have meant to be unkind, and she certainly didn't mean any harm. It was a small culture clash, and a smile soon smoothed it over.

1. What did Pricila first deduce when the man went quiet?
A That she must have done something wrong.
B That he would help her.
C That nothing happened.
2. What does "You can't have known the rule" mean?
A She definitely knew the rule.
B It's almost impossible that she knew it.
C She must learn it now.
3. How is the moment resolved in the end?
A With an argument.
B She never speaks to him again.
C A smile smooths it over -- just a culture clash.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Past Modals of Deduction

Grammar

Os modais de deducao no passado mostram o quanto voce tem CERTEZA sobre o passado: must have + particio = quase certo que SIM ("She must have misunderstood me"); can't have + particio = quase certo que NAO ("They can't have meant it" -- NUNCA "mustn't have"); might/could have + particio = era possivel. A forma nao muda para he/she/it (NAO "must has"). E "must have", nao "must of". / Past modals of deduction show how sure you are about the past: must have + past participle = almost sure it was true; can't have + past participle = almost sure it was NOT true (never "mustn't have"); might/could have = it was possible. The form never changes for he/she/it, and it is "must have", not "must of".

Funcao / FunctionEnglishPortugues
Almost sure it WAS trueShe must have misunderstood me.Ela deve ter me entendido mal.
It was possibleHe might have been confused by the custom.Ele pode ter ficado confuso com o costume.
Almost sure it was NOT trueThey can't have meant it rudely.Eles nao podem ter feito por grosseria.
Passive deductionThe card must have been offered with one hand.O cartao deve ter sido oferecido com uma mao so.
Common mistakeNOT "must of" / NOT "mustn't have"E "must have"; para certeza negativa, use "can't have".

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"She looked confused -- she must misunderstood me."
"He was smiling, so he have been angry."
"They went quiet -- they have a different custom."
"It was a faux pas -- I must have a local rule."
"The card must have offered with one hand."
"Don't jump to -- give them the benefit of the doubt."

Stage 2: Order the Misunderstanding

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of Pricila's misunderstanding in order.

?Then she worries: she must have done something wrong.
?So she smooths things over with a warm smile, and the awkward silence melts away.
?First, Pricila bows to a colleague in Japan, and he looks surprised.
?After that, she sees he meant no harm, and she gives him the benefit of the doubt.
?Next, a friend explains the local custom, so she realizes she can't have known the rule.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Keep a calm, thoughtful voice.

She must have misunderstood me.
Ela deve ter me entendido mal.
They can't have meant any harm.
Eles nao podem ter tido ma intencao.
It must have been a culture clash.
Deve ter sido um choque cultural.
A smile usually smooths things over.
Um sorriso geralmente contorna a situacao.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

A colleague went silent and you're almost sure you broke a custom. What do you say?
A "I must of broken a custom."
B "I must have broken a custom."
C "I must broke a custom."
You're almost sure the waiter was NOT being rude. How do you say it?
A "He can't have been rude -- it was a culture clash."
B "He mustn't have been rude."
C "He don't have been rude."
A friend assumes the worst about a stranger's silence. What do you advise?
A "Jump to conclusions right away."
B "Read between the lines and give them the benefit of the doubt."
C "Make them lose face in public."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think of a confusing moment with a stranger -- abroad or at home. Deduce what they must have meant, what they might have felt, and why they can't have meant any harm. Finish with how you would give them the benefit of the doubt. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 18

1She must have misunderstood me.Ela deve ter me entendido mal.
2They can't have meant any harm.Eles nao podem ter tido ma intencao.
3It must have been a culture clash.Deve ter sido um choque cultural.
4I'll give them the benefit of the doubt.Vou dar a eles o beneficio da duvida.
5A smile usually smooths things over.Um sorriso geralmente contorna a situacao.
Aula 19 -- Pre-class

Habits, Hobbies & the Life You Want

Fale dos seus habitos, hobbies e da vida que voce quer construir, usando gerundios e infinitivos (I enjoy painting / I decided to take up the piano). Aprenda quando usar -ing e quando usar to + verbo.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Habits & Hobbies Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These expressions help you talk about hobbies and the life you want.

To take up -- comecar (um hobby/atividade)
"At sixty, she decided to take up watercolour painting."
To give up -- largar / abandonar (um habito)
"I gave up scrolling on my phone every evening."
To be into something -- curtir / ser fa de algo
"I'm really into birdwatching these days."
To get the hang of -- pegar o jeito de
"It took a while, but I got the hang of the piano."
A pastime -- um passatempo
"Painting is the pastime that helps me relax."
To unwind -- relaxar / descontrair
"I read for an hour to unwind before bed."
To keep up with -- acompanhar / nao ficar para tras
"I couldn't keep up with my running routine, so I stopped."
A creature of habit -- pessoa de habitos / rotineira
"I'm a creature of habit -- coffee at seven, walk at eight."
To make time for -- arrumar tempo para
"However busy I am, I make time for my Saturday class."
To be hooked on -- estar viciado em / fissurado
"I'm completely hooked on gardening now."
To dabble in -- experimentar sem se aprofundar
"I dabble in cooking -- nothing fancy, just for fun."
An acquired taste -- um gosto que se adquire com o tempo
"Olives were an acquired taste, but now I love them."
To broaden your horizons -- ampliar seus horizontes
"Trying new things broadens your horizons, even at sixty."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

To take up
To give up
To be into something
To get the hang of
A pastime
To unwind
To keep up with
A creature of habit
To make time for
To be hooked on
To dabble in
An acquired taste
To broaden your horizons

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story and answer the questions.

For years, Pricila was a creature of habit. She would finish work and just watch television. "I enjoy relaxing," she told herself, "but I never make time for a real hobby." One day she decided to take up painting. At first she couldn't stand making such a mess, and she almost gave up. But she kept practising, and slowly she got the hang of it. Now she's hooked. "Painting helps me unwind," she says, "and I'm planning to learn the piano next. Trying new things really broadens your horizons."

1. After "I enjoy", which form is correct?
A "I enjoy relaxing." (-ing)
B "I enjoy to relax." (to + verb)
C "I enjoy relax." (base verb)
2. After "she decided", which form is correct?
A "She decided taking up painting."
B "She decided to take up painting."
C "She decided take up painting."
3. What does the story say happened after she kept practising?
A She gave up for good.
B She never improved.
C She slowly got the hang of it and is now hooked.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Gerunds vs Infinitives

Grammar

Alguns verbos pedem o -ing (gerundio); outros pedem to + verbo (infinitivo). Verbo + -ing: enjoy, avoid, keep, give up, can't stand ("I enjoy painting"). Verbo + to + verbo: decide, manage, hope, plan, afford ("I decided to take up the piano"). Depois de PREPOSICAO use sempre -ing ("good at planning", "interested in learning"). Como SUJEITO, use -ing ("Travelling broadens the mind"). Alguns mudam de sentido: stop doing = parar a atividade; stop to do = parar PARA fazer; remember doing = lembrar do passado; remember to do = nao esquecer no futuro. / Some verbs take -ing, others take to + verb. After a preposition, always use -ing. As a subject, use -ing. A few verbs change meaning (stop doing vs stop to do; remember doing vs remember to do).

Pattern / PadraoEnglishPortugues
verb + -ing (enjoy, keep, give up)I enjoy painting.Eu gosto de pintar.
verb + to + base (decide, plan, afford)I decided to take up the piano.Decidi comecar a tocar piano.
after a prepositionI'm good at planning.Eu sou bom em planejar.
as the subjectTravelling broadens the mind.Viajar amplia a mente.
meaning changestop doing ≠ stop to doparar a atividade ≠ parar para fazer algo.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with -ing or to + verb. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"I really enjoy in the mornings."
"She decided up the piano."
"I'm interested in to cook."
"We can't afford our weekends."
" broadens the mind."
"Remember time for what you love."

Stage 2: Order the Hobby Story

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of Pricila's hobby story in order.

?Then she decided to take up watercolour painting instead.
?So now painting is the pastime that helps her unwind and broadens her horizons.
?First, Pricila gave up jogging, because she couldn't keep up with it.
?After that, she found she was really into it, and started to make time for it every weekend.
?Next, she dabbled in it for a few weeks, and slowly got the hang of it.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Keep a calm, natural voice.

I really enjoy painting.
Eu gosto muito de pintar.
I've decided to take up a new hobby.
Decidi comecar um hobby novo.
I make time for what I love.
Eu arrumo tempo para o que eu amo.
Trying new things broadens your horizons.
Experimentar coisas novas amplia seus horizontes.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

A friend asks what you like doing in your free time. How do you answer?
A "I enjoy to paint and to read."
B "I enjoy painting and reading."
C "I enjoy paint and read."
You made a firm choice to start the piano. How do you say it?
A "I decided to take up the piano."
B "I decided taking up the piano."
C "I decided take up the piano."
You want to say you are skilled at organising your week. How?
A "I'm good at to plan my week."
B "I'm good at plan my week."
C "I'm good at planning my week."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Talk about the life you want to build. What do you enjoy doing? What have you decided to take up, or planned to give up? How do you make time for the things you're into? Use -ing and to + verb naturally. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 19

1I really enjoy painting.Eu gosto muito de pintar.
2I've decided to take up a new hobby.Decidi comecar um hobby novo.
3I'm really into it now.Estou curtindo muito isso agora.
4I make time for what I love.Eu arrumo tempo para o que eu amo.
5Trying new things broadens your horizons.Experimentar coisas novas amplia seus horizontes.
Aula 20 -- Pre-class

Getting Things Sorted Abroad

Resolva a vida real morando ou viajando no exterior: marque servicos e arranje tudo (get things sorted) usando a causativa -- have/get something done (I had my hair cut / I need to get my documents translated). A aula final da sua jornada.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Settling-In Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These expressions help you arrange services and settle in abroad.

To settle in -- instalar-se / se ambientar
"It took a month to settle in, but now it feels like home."
To get something sorted -- resolver / dar um jeito em algo
"It was a busy week, but I got everything sorted in the end."
An errand -- uma tarefa / um recado (fora de casa)
"I have a few errands to run before the shops close."
A service provider -- um prestador de servico
"The internet service provider sent a technician the next day."
To book an appointment -- marcar um horario
"I booked an appointment at the salon for Friday morning."
To drop something off -- deixar algo (para um servico)
"I'll drop the coat off at the dry cleaner's on my way."
A quote -- um orcamento
"The garage gave me a fair quote before fixing the car."
A deposit -- um sinal / deposito (adiantamento)
"They asked for a small deposit to confirm the appointment."
To get something fixed -- consertar algo (mandar consertar)
"I need to get the heater fixed before winter."
Red tape -- burocracia
"There was a lot of red tape before I could open a bank account."
To sign up for -- inscrever-se / contratar (um servico)
"I signed up for a local SIM card and a gym membership."
To set something up -- configurar / instalar algo
"The technician set up the internet in about an hour."
Paperwork -- papelada / documentacao
"There was a mountain of paperwork to sign at the office."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

To settle in
To get something sorted
An errand
A service provider
To book an appointment
To drop something off
A quote
A deposit
To get something fixed
Red tape
To sign up for
To set something up
Paperwork

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story and answer the questions.

Pricila's first week abroad was all about getting things sorted. On Monday she had the locks changed and got the internet set up. There was a lot of red tape, so she had a few documents translated for the paperwork. On Wednesday she booked an appointment and had her hair cut. She also dropped her coat off and got it cleaned. "I didn't do any of it myself," she laughs. "I just arranged it all. And honestly, once you've had things fixed in your own name, a strange place starts to feel like home."

1. "She had her hair cut." Who cut Pricila's hair?
A Pricila cut it herself.
B A hairdresser did it for her -- she arranged the service.
C Nobody -- her hair is still long.
2. Which form is correct for the causative?
A "She had the locks to change."
B "She had the locks changed." (have + object + past participle)
C "She had changed the locks." (= she changed them herself, earlier)
3. What does the story say makes a strange place feel like home?
A Doing every single job by yourself.
B Avoiding all the red tape and paperwork.
C Having things fixed and sorted in your own name.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Causative (have/get something done)

Grammar

Usamos a CAUSATIVA quando ARRANJAMOS que outra pessoa faca um servico para nos, em vez de fazer nos mesmos. Forma: have/get + OBJETO + particio passado. Compare: "I cut my hair" (eu cortei) vs "I had my hair cut" (o cabeleireiro cortou para mim). A parte have/get muda com o tempo verbal (had / am having / will have / am going to get), mas o segundo verbo e SEMPRE o particio passado (cut, translated, painted, fixed, set up). 'Have' e 'get' tem o mesmo sentido aqui; 'get' e um pouco mais informal. Ha tambem a causativa de EXPERIENCIA, para coisas ruins que acontecem COM voce: "She had her bag stolen" (roubaram a bolsa dela). NAO confunda com a voz passiva (Aula 16): na causativa VOCE continua sendo quem arranja o servico. / The causative = have/get + object + past participle, used when you arrange for someone else to do a job. The second verb is always the past participle. There is also an experience causative (She had her bag stolen). It is different from the plain passive: in the causative, YOU arranged it.

Uso / UseEnglishPortugues
You do it yourselfI cut my hair.Eu cortei meu cabelo.
You arrange a serviceI had my hair cut.Cortei o cabelo (mandei cortar).
Service (informal, get)I got the car fixed.Mandei consertar o carro.
Experience (it happens to you)She had her bag stolen.Roubaram a bolsa dela.
Different from passiveThe car was fixed ≠ I got the car fixedO carro foi consertado ≠ eu mandei consertar.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the past participle. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"I had my hair yesterday."
"I'm going to get my documents ."
"We're having our apartment this week."
"I need to get the car ."
"She had her bag at the station."
"I'm going to have the internet up tomorrow."

Stage 2: Order the Settling-In Story

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of Pricila's settling-in story in order.

?Then she had the locks changed and got the internet set up, after a little red tape with the paperwork.
?So now, with everything sorted, she feels completely at home abroad.
?First, Pricila settled into her new apartment, but there was a lot to get sorted.
?After that, she dropped her winter coat off at the dry cleaner's and had it cleaned.
?Next, she booked an appointment at a salon and finally had her hair cut the way she likes it.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Keep a calm, natural voice.

I'd like to get my hair cut.
Eu gostaria de cortar o cabelo (mandar cortar).
Can I book an appointment?
Posso marcar um horario?
I need to get the internet set up.
Preciso configurar a internet (mandar configurar).
I'll get it all sorted today.
Vou dar um jeito em tudo hoje.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

A hairdresser cut your hair last week. How do you say it?
A "I cut my hair last week."
B "I had my hair cut last week."
C "I had my hair to cut last week."
You want a mechanic to repair your car. How do you say it?
A "I need to get the car fixed."
B "I need to get fix the car."
C "I need to get the car to fix."
Someone stole your phone yesterday (an experience). How do you say it?
A "I had stolen my phone yesterday."
B "I had my phone to steal yesterday."
C "I had my phone stolen yesterday."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine your first week living abroad. What did you have done or get sorted to settle in -- the hair, the car, the documents, the internet? What red tape did you deal with? Use the causative (have/get something done) naturally. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 20

1I'd like to get my hair cut.Eu gostaria de cortar o cabelo (mandar cortar).
2Can I book an appointment?Posso marcar um horario?
3Could I get a quote first?Posso ter um orcamento antes?
4I need to get the internet set up.Preciso configurar a internet.
5I'll get it all sorted today.Vou dar um jeito em tudo hoje.

IN CLASS -- Select Lesson

01
Who Is Pricila Adamo?
Diagnostic + Personal Story -- 37 slides
02
My Life in Chapters
Past Experiences & Present Reality -- 37 slides
03
The Retirement Dream
Plans & Goals -- 37 slides
04
A Life of Travel
Describing Places & Experiences -- 37 slides
05
Health and Wellness
Self-Care Language -- 37 slides
06
At the Airport
Checking In & Boarding -- 29 slides
07
At the Hotel
Reservations & requests (would like / need to / have to) -- 29 slides
08
Getting Around
Directions + prepositions of place -- 29 slides
09
Eating Out
Ordering language + some/any + dietary -- 29 slides
10
Shopping Abroad
Quantifiers, prices, too/enough -- 29 slides
11
Small Talk
Question forms & tag questions -- 28 slides
12
Sharing Opinions
Agreeing & disagreeing politely -- 28 slides
13
Making Plans & Invitations
Inviting & arranging to meet -- 28 slides
14
Phone & Messages
Calling, taking messages & arranging by phone -- 28 slides
15
Asking for Help & Emergencies
Reporting problems & getting help calmly -- 28 slides
16
Banking & Money Abroad
Exchanging money & asking politely at the bank -- 28 slides
17
Looking Back, Moving Forward
Past regrets & the third conditional -- 35 slides
18
Reading People & Situations
Deducing the past: must/might/can't have -- 35 slides
19
Habits, Hobbies & the Life You Want
Gerunds vs infinitives -- verb patterns -- 35 slides
20
Getting Things Sorted Abroad
The causative: have/get something done -- 35 slides

Materiais Complementares -- Aula 1

Lesson 1 — Who Is Pricila Adamo?

Filme
Eat Pray Love (2010)

Viagem, autoconhecimento e transição de vida — altamente relevante para a fase da Pricila. Julia Roberts viaja pelo mundo após uma grande mudança. Inglês claro, ritmo acessível.

Dica: assista os primeiros 30 minutos com áudio em inglês e legenda em inglês. Anote 5 palavras novas.

Disponível nos streamings: Netflix e Max

Podcast
Hidden Brain — The Paradox of Pleasure

A psiquiatra Anna Lembke (autora de 'Dopamine Nation') explica como prazer e dor funcionam como uma gangorra no cérebro e por que a abundância moderna nos deixa ansiosos e deprimidos. Psicologia e comportamento em inglês americano claro.

Ouça um trecho por vez e anote o vocabulário de comportamento (addiction, balance, reward).

Open episode ↗

YouTube
Hardcore History -- Dan Carlin (Preview Clips)

Narrativas históricas envolventes em inglês americano. Dan Carlin conta histórias com paixão — perfeito para quem ama história como a Pricila.

Dica: comece pelos clipes de 10-15 minutos no YouTube. Foque no ritmo e nas expressões, não em entender 100%.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 2 — My Life in Chapters

Série
The Crown -- Season 1, Episode 1 (Netflix)

História, cultura e inglês claro com sotaque variado. A narrativa é cronológica — perfeita para praticar Past Simple em contexto real. Pricila ama história.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e anote 3 frases no Past Simple que descrevem eventos importantes.

Disponível no streaming: Netflix

Podcast
Stuff You Missed in History Class — How the Rosetta Stone Works

Episodio que conta como a Pedra de Roseta permitiu decifrar os hieroglifos egipcios -- um verdadeiro marco (milestone) da historia. Ingles americano acessivel, em narrativa cronologica.

Preste atencao as datas e a ordem cronologica dos eventos.

Open episode ↗

YouTube
Freakonomics Radio -- "How to Change Your Mind"

Episódio sobre como experiências transformadoras mudam nossa perspectiva. Vocabulário rico em "reflect", "transform", "overcome". Inglês americano claro.

Dica: ouça os primeiros 10 minutos e anote 3 momentos onde o apresentador fala sobre experiências passadas.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 3 — The Retirement Dream

Filme
The Best Exotic Marigold Hotel (2011)

Um grupo de aposentados britânicos viaja para a Índia buscando uma nova vida. Temas de aposentadoria, aventura, abraçar mudanças e encontrar propósito — perfeito para a fase da Pricila.

Dica: assista os primeiros 40 minutos com legenda em inglês. Anote como os personagens falam sobre seus planos usando "going to" e "will".

Disponível nos streamings: Netflix e Disney+

Podcast
Hidden Brain — You 2.0: WOOP, WOOP

A psicóloga Gabriele Oettingen mostra por que apenas sonhar alto atrapalha alcançar objetivos e apresenta o método WOOP (Wish, Outcome, Obstacle, Plan). Vocabulário rico em goals, dreams, wishes e purpose, em inglês americano claro.

Pause após cada etapa do WOOP e tente descrever um objetivo seu em voz alta.

Open episode ↗

Série
Explained (Netflix/Vox) -- "Retirement" Episode

Episódio curto sobre aposentadoria ao redor do mundo. Vocabulário de lifestyle, freedom, priorities. Inglês americano B2, ritmo acessível.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e identifique 5 palavras do vocabulário desta aula.

Disponível no streaming: Netflix

Lesson 4 — A Life of Travel

Documentário
Our Planet -- Episode 1 (Netflix)

Narrado por David Attenborough. Imagens de tirar o fôlego da natureza com vocabulário descritivo rico: stunning, breathtaking, remote, ancient. Inglês britânico claro.

Dica: assista 20 minutos e anote todos os adjetivos que o narrador usa para descrever paisagens.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Podcast
Stuff You Missed in History Class — How the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World Work

Episodio sobre as Sete Maravilhas do Mundo Antigo, lugares monumentais e sua historia (incluindo se os Jardins Suspensos da Babilonia existiram). Rico em adjetivos descritivos e vocabulario de lugares antigos.

Anote os adjetivos usados para descrever cada maravilha (ancient, stunning).

Open episode ↗

YouTube
Rick Steves' Europe -- Best Travel Moments

Rick Steves descreve destinos usando adjetivos ricos e comparações. Inglês americano claro, ritmo acessível. Ótimo modelo de como descrever lugares.

Dica: assista 15 minutos e anote como ele compara cidades usando "more... than" e "the most..."

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 5 — Health and Wellness

Documentário
Heal (2017) -- Netflix/Amazon

Documentário sobre a conexão mente-corpo e o poder da cura. Vocabulário rico em wellness, recovery, mindful, prevent. Diretamente conectado ao interesse da Pricila em saúde e psicanálise.

Dica: assista 30 minutos com legenda em inglês e anote os conselhos que os especialistas dão usando "should", "could" e "might".

Streaming no Brasil: verificar disponibilidade

Podcast
Hidden Brain — You 2.0: The Path to Contentment

A psicóloga Iris Mauss explica por que perseguir a felicidade de forma obsessiva sai pela culatra e como a aceitação leva ao equilíbrio e à vida boa. Vocabulário de wellness, balance, happiness e habits, em inglês americano acessível.

Repita em voz alta as frases de preferência ('I prefer to feel...') para praticar entonação natural.

Open episode ↗

TED Talk
What makes a good life? -- Robert Waldinger

TED Talk sobre o que realmente importa para a saúde e felicidade a longo prazo. Vocabulário de wellness, balance, habits, relationships. Inglês claro e inspirador.

Dica: assista com legenda em inglês e anote 3 conselhos que o palestrante dá. Reformule usando "You should..." e "You could..."

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 6 — At the Airport

Filme
The Terminal (2004) — Tom Hanks, dir. Steven Spielberg

O filme inteiro se passa em um aeroporto. Viktor (Tom Hanks) precisa resolver tudo no balcão e nos portões falando inglês. Preste atenção em pedidos educados como "Could you help me?" e "I would like to..." — exatamente as estruturas da aula.

Dica: Assista 15 minutos com legendas em inglês. Anote toda vez que alguém faz um pedido educado (Could I / Could you / Would you mind).

Disponível no streaming: Paramount+

Podcast
All Ears English — AEE 2297: Traveling Soon? English Phrases for the Airport

Episódio real (out/2024, 21 min) com as frases mais úteis no aeroporto: check-in, bagagem, portão e atrasos, com pedidos educados em contexto real.

Ouça uma vez sem pausar e depois repita as frases-chave imitando a entonação.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
"How to Speak English at the Airport (Listen & Repeat)"

Vídeo de listen-and-repeat com as frases exatas que você precisa no check-in e no embarque: "Could I have a window seat?", "I would like to check one bag", "Where is the gate?". Repita em voz alta com o vídeo.

Dica: Pause depois de cada frase e repita imitando a entonação. Foque nos pedidos com "Could" e "Would you mind".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 7 — At the Hotel

Filme
The Grand Budapest Hotel (2014) — dir. Wes Anderson

O filme inteiro se passa em um hotel. Observe o concierge e a recepção atendendo hóspedes: pedidos, reclamações e serviços. Repare em frases como "I would like..." e "We have to..." — a gramática da aula em ação.

Dica: Assista 15 minutos com legendas em inglês. Anote cada vez que um hóspede pede algo ou faz uma reclamação.

Disponível nos streamings: Netflix e Disney+

Podcast
English Vocabulary Help — Learn Essential English Phrases for Staying in a Hotel

Episódio com as frases essenciais para o hotel: fazer o check-in, falar com a recepção e pedir serviços. Você ouve "I have a reservation...", "Do you have...?" e "What time...?" em contexto real.

Ouça e repita cada frase em voz alta simulando um check-in na recepção.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
"How to Check in at a Hotel in English (Travel English Lesson)"

Vídeo com a conversa completa de check-in: pedir o quarto, mostrar a reserva, perguntar sobre o café da manhã e o check-out. Repita as frases em voz alta com o vídeo.

Dica: Pause depois de cada frase e repita. Foque em "I would like to...", "I need to..." e "have to".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 8 — Getting Around

Filme
Paddington (2014) — dir. Paul King

O ursinho Paddington chega a Londres e precisa se virar na cidade: o metrô (the Underground), mapas, direções e perguntas a estranhos. Observe frases como "How do I get to...?" e direções com imperativos.

Dica: Assista 15 minutos com legendas em inglês. Anote toda direção e preposição de lugar (next to, across from, opposite).

Streaming no Brasil: verificar disponibilidade

Podcast
All Ears English — AEE 2297: Traveling Soon? English Phrases for the Airport

Episódio de viagem com frases reais para o aeroporto: check-in, embarque, segurança e como pedir informação sobre portões e voos. Vocabulário prático que também ajuda a se orientar e perguntar o caminho num contexto de viagem.

Ouça uma vez para entender e, na segunda, repita as frases-chave em voz alta.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
"Public Transport Conversations: Bus, Subway & Taxi Basics"

Vídeo com diálogos reais no ônibus, no metrô e no táxi: comprar bilhete, perguntar a plataforma, e onde descer. Repita as frases em voz alta com o vídeo.

Dica: Pause depois de cada frase e repita. Foque em "How do I get to...?", "Take the... line" e "get off at...".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 9 — Eating Out

Filme
Ratatouille (2007) — Pixar / Disney

O filme se passa quase todo em um restaurante em Paris. Observe o cardápio, o garçom, os pedidos e o crítico fazendo seu pedido. Ótimo para vocabulário de starter, main course, dessert e bill.

Dica: Assista a cena do crítico (Anton Ego) no restaurante com legendas em inglês. Anote como ele pede e como o garçom responde.

Disponível no streaming: Disney+

Podcast
All Ears English — 4 English Phrases to Start a Meal

Episódio sobre a hora da refeição: frases naturais para começar a comer, interagir à mesa e ser educado num jantar ou almoço, útil para situações sociais e de negócios num restaurante.

Anote as 4 frases e use uma na próxima vez que sentar para comer.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
"Ordering Food at a Restaurant — English Conversation"

Vídeo com a conversa completa no restaurante: pedir o cardápio, escolher entrada e prato principal, perguntar sobre ingredientes e pedir a conta. Repita as frases em voz alta.

Dica: Pause depois de cada frase e repita. Foque em "I will have...", "Is there any...?" e "Could we have the bill?".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 10 — Shopping Abroad

Filme
Aladdin (2019) — Disney

O filme começa num grande mercado (the marketplace) cheio de barracas, preços e pechincha. Observe como os vendedores anunciam e como os personagens negociam. Ótimo para vocabulário de market, stall, bargain e price.

Dica: Assista às cenas do mercado com legendas em inglês. Anote como falam de preços e descontos.

Disponível no streaming: Disney+

Podcast
Real Easy English (Learning Easy English) — Talking about shopping

Conversa lenta e clara em inglês fácil sobre fazer compras, com preços, escolhas e a pergunta 'How much...?'. Você ouve 'too expensive' e linguagem do dia a dia.

Repita 'How much is it?' e 'It's too expensive.' em voz alta enquanto ouve.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
"Bargaining in English: Ask for Better Prices"

Vídeo com frases para perguntar preços e negociar: "How much is this?", "That is too expensive", "Can you give me a discount?". Repita em voz alta com o vídeo.

Dica: Pause depois de cada frase e repita. Foque em "too / enough" e em pedir desconto educadamente.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 11 — Small Talk

Serie / Filme
"Before Sunrise" (1995) -- two strangers start talking on a train

Um classico sobre dois desconhecidos que comecam a conversar em um trem na Europa. Modelo perfeito de small talk natural entre viajantes. Conexao com a aula: quebrar o gelo e manter a conversa fluindo.

Dica: assista a cena de abertura com legenda em ingles. Anote 3 perguntas que eles usam para se conhecer.

Streaming no Brasil: verificar disponibilidade

Podcast
BBC Learning English — On the fly (The English We Speak)

Episodio curto da serie The English We Speak que ensina a expressao informal 'on the fly' (fazer algo de improviso, sem planejar), com um dialogo do dia a dia sobre cozinhar sem receita.

Dica: repita a expressao em voz alta e crie uma frase sua usando 'on the fly'.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
Question tags - 6 Minute Grammar

Video que explica como formar e pronunciar tag questions, com a entonacao certa (subindo para perguntar, descendo para confirmar). Revisao direta do foco da aula.

Dica: pause o video e crie 5 tag questions sobre a sua proxima viagem.

Assistir no YouTube ↗

Lesson 12 — Sharing Opinions

Serie / Filme
"Chef's Table" (Netflix) -- chefs share strong opinions about food & culture

Cada episodio e um chef defendendo, com paixao e razoes, sua visao sobre comida e viagem. Modelo perfeito de "In my opinion... because...". Conexao com a aula: dar opinioes com argumentos.

Dica: assista 10 minutos com legenda em ingles. Anote 3 opinioes do chef e a razao de cada uma.

Disponível no streaming: Netflix

Podcast
BBC Learning English — Is it OK to disagree? (6 Minute English)

Episodio de 6 Minute English em que Neil e Beth discutem o desacordo e a polarizacao social, ensinando vocabulario para concordar e discordar em ingles natural.

Dica: anote as expressoes de opiniao que aparecem e tente reutiliza-las depois.

Listen on Spotify ↗

YouTube
Eat Pray Love — Official Trailer

Trailer oficial de Eat Pray Love (2010): dialogo natural e vocabulario de viagem, autoconhecimento e relacionamentos, otimo para treinar registro coloquial e escuta em ritmo real.

Assista com legendas em ingles e repita em voz alta as falas da Julia Roberts para imitar entonacao e ritmo.

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 13 — Making Plans & Invitations

Serie / Filme
"Friends" (Netflix) -- the group is always making plans & inviting each other

Em quase todo episodio alguem convida os amigos para o cafe, um jantar ou um passeio. Modelo natural de "Do you want to...?", "How about...?" e "Let's...". Conexao com a aula: convidar e combinar programas.

Dica: assista 10 minutos com legenda em ingles e anote 3 maneiras diferentes que eles usam para convidar.

Disponível no streaming: Max

Podcast
BBC Learning English — Setting an agenda (English at Work, ep. 20)

Episodio da serie English at Work em que a equipe da Tip Top Trading organiza uma reuniao: como definir a pauta, sugerir topicos e combinar os proximos passos.

Dica: ouca prestando atencao nas frases usadas para marcar e estruturar reunioes.

Watch on YouTube ↗

YouTube
Making, Accepting & Declining an Invitation in English
