Video que ensina a convidar, sugerir, aceitar e recusar planos com educacao, com exemplos do dia a dia. Revisao direta do foco da aula.

Dica: pause o video e escreva 5 convites para a sua proxima viagem, cada um com um horario e um lugar.

Assistir no YouTube ↗

Lesson 14 — Phone & Messages

Serie / Filme
"The Office" (US) -- the front desk & constant phone calls

Receptionists e colegas atendem o telefone, anotam recados e dizem "Can I take a message?" o tempo todo. Modelo natural de linguagem de telefone em ambiente real. Conexao com a aula: atender, anotar recado e passar a informacao adiante.

Dica: assista 10 minutos com legenda em ingles e anote 3 frases que usam para atender ou anotar um recado.

Disponível nos streamings: Netflix, Prime Video e Max

Podcast
BBC Learning English — Taking telephone messages (English at Work, ep. 53)

Episodio da serie English at Work sobre atender o telefone e anotar recados no trabalho, com frases para pedir dados, confirmar e prometer retorno.

Dica: pratique anotar nome, telefone e recado enquanto ouve o dialogo.

Watch on YouTube ↗

YouTube
Talking on the Phone in English - English Phone Vocabulary Lesson

Video que ensina a comecar uma ligacao ("I'm calling to..."), anotar e deixar recados, e pedir para retornarem, com exemplos do dia a dia. Revisao direta do foco da aula.

Dica: pause o video e escreva 5 motivos de ligacao com "I'm calling to...", cada um para um lugar diferente da sua viagem.

Assistir no YouTube ↗

Lesson 15 — Asking for Help & Emergencies

Serie / Filme
"Brooklyn Nine-Nine" -- police station, reporting crimes

As pessoas chegam a delegacia para reportar roubos e problemas, e os policiais perguntam o que aconteceu, onde e quando. Modelo natural de linguagem para reportar uma emergencia. Conexao com a aula: "Someone has taken...", "I need to report...".

Dica: assista 10 minutos com legenda em ingles e anote 3 frases usadas para reportar um problema.

Disponível no streaming: Netflix

Podcast
BBC Learning English — Falling for fraud (6 Minute English)

Episodio de 6 Minute English sobre golpes e fraudes: como criminosos enganam as pessoas e o vocabulario para falar de roubo, batedores de carteira e como se proteger — util em viagens.

Dica: liste as palavras novas sobre golpes e roubo e revise antes de viajar.

Watch on YouTube ↗

YouTube
10 Phrases to Help You in an Emergency in English

Video que ensina a pedir ajuda ("Could you help me?"), reportar roubos e perdas ("Someone has taken...", "I've lost...") e encontrar uma farmacia, com exemplos do dia a dia. Revisao direta do foco da aula.

Dica: pause o video e escreva 5 problemas de viagem com "Someone has taken..." ou "I've lost...".

Assistir no YouTube ↗

Lesson 16 — Banking & Money Abroad

Serie / Filme
"An Idiot Abroad" -- exchanging money & haggling abroad

Karl viaja por varios paises e precisa lidar com moeda estrangeira, casas de cambio e dinheiro o tempo todo. Modelo natural de linguagem de dinheiro em viagem. Conexao com a aula: trocar moeda, perguntar a taxa e o fee.

Dica: assista 10 minutos com legenda em ingles e anote 3 frases usadas para falar de dinheiro ou trocar moeda.

Streaming no Brasil: verificar disponibilidade

Podcast
BBC Learning English — Asking Questions (6 Minute Grammar)

Episodio de 6 Minute Grammar que revisa como formar perguntas em ingles, incluindo maneiras mais educadas e indiretas de pedir informacoes — base para perguntar sobre cambio e taxas no banco.

Dica: pratique transformar perguntas diretas em versoes mais educadas ('Could you tell me...?').

Watch on YouTube ↗

YouTube
Exchange Money in English | Currency Exchange | Travel English

Video que ensina a trocar moeda, sacar dinheiro e fazer perguntas indiretas educadas ("Could you tell me where the ATM is?"), com exemplos do dia a dia. Revisao direta do foco da aula.

Dica: pause o video e escreva 5 perguntas indiretas que voce faria no banco, usando "Could you tell me..." e "Do you know if...".

Assistir no YouTube ↗

Lesson 17 — Looking Back, Moving Forward

Serie / Filme
"The Secret Life of Walter Mitty" -- looking back & leaps of faith

Walter olha para uma vida sem aventuras e decide, num salto de fe, viver de verdade. Cheio de momentos de reflexao e turning points. Conexao com a aula: refletir sobre escolhas e o que teria sido diferente.

Dica: assista uma cena com legenda em ingles e anote 3 momentos que sao "turning points" na vida do personagem.

Disponível nos streamings: Disney+, Max, Prime Video e Globoplay

Podcast
BBC Learning English — How to use the third conditional (6 Minute Grammar)

Episodio de 6 Minute Grammar que pratica o terceiro condicional ('If I had known, I would have...') para falar de situacoes passadas hipoteticas e arrependimentos.

Dica: crie 3 frases sobre coisas que voce teria feito diferente no passado.

Watch on YouTube ↗

YouTube
"Third Conditional & I Wish -- Talking About Past Regrets"

Video que ensina a formar o terceiro condicional e a usar "I wish I had..." e "If only I had..." para falar de arrependimentos, com exemplos do dia a dia. Revisao direta do foco da aula.

Dica: pause o video e escreva 5 frases sobre coisas que voce teria feito diferente, usando "If I had..., I would have...".

Watch on YouTube ↗

Lesson 18 — Reading People & Situations

Serie / Filme
"Lost in Translation" -- a culture clash in Tokyo

Dois estrangeiros tentam entender costumes japoneses e a linguagem corporal de quem os cerca. Cheio de mal-entendidos e silencios constrangedores. Conexao com a aula: deduzir o que as pessoas devem ter querido dizer num pais diferente.

Dica: assista uma cena com legenda em ingles e anote 3 momentos em que um personagem "must have" interpretado mal a situacao.

Disponível no streaming: Netflix

Podcast
BBC Learning English — Modals of deduction and speculation (6 Minute Grammar)

Episodio de 6 Minute Grammar sobre modais de deducao e especulacao (must, might, could, can't), incluindo como deduzir o que aconteceu.

Dica: olhe uma foto e faca deducoes usando 'must have' e 'might have'.

Watch on YouTube ↗

YouTube
Gestures Around the World

Video que mostra como gestos, distancia e linguagem corporal mudam de pais para pais -- e como evitar uma gafe (faux pas) sem querer. Revisao direta do vocabulario da aula.

Dica: pause o video e escreva 5 frases sobre o que um viajante "might have" feito errado, usando os modais de deducao.

Assistir no YouTube ↗

Lesson 19 — Habits, Hobbies & the Life You Want

Serie / Filme
"Julie & Julia" -- taking up a new pastime later in life

Duas mulheres descobrem a cozinha como hobby e deixam a rotina para tras. Cheio de "I decided to...", "I enjoy...", "I gave up...". Conexao com a aula: falar do hobby que voce decide comecar e o que ele muda na sua vida.

Dica: assista uma cena com legenda em ingles e anote 5 frases com -ing ou to + verbo sobre o que a personagem gosta de fazer.

Streaming no Brasil: verificar disponibilidade

Podcast
BBC Learning English — Verb patterns: gerunds and infinitives (6 Minute Grammar)

Episodio de 6 Minute Grammar sobre padroes verbais: quando um verbo e seguido de gerundio (-ing) e quando e seguido de infinitivo (to + verbo), e como o sentido pode mudar.

Dica: memorize os verbos por grupo (enjoy doing, decide to do, stop doing vs stop to do).

Watch on YouTube ↗

YouTube
Top 10 Hobbies You Should Pick Up in Retirement

Video que mostra pessoas que decidiram comecar um novo hobby mais tarde na vida -- pintura, jardinagem, musica -- e como isso ajuda a relaxar (unwind) e a manter a mente curiosa. Revisao direta do vocabulario da aula.

Dica: pause o video e escreva 5 frases sobre o que voce gostaria de take up ou give up, usando -ing e to + verbo.

Assistir no YouTube ↗

Lesson 20 — Getting Things Sorted Abroad

Serie / Filme
"Emily in Paris" -- settling into life in a new country

Uma americana se muda para Paris e precisa resolver tudo: apartamento, burocracia, servicos, idioma. Cheio de situacoes de "getting things sorted" no exterior. Conexao com a aula: observe como ela arranja servicos e lida com a red tape de outro pais.

Dica: assista uma cena com legenda em ingles e anote 5 servicos que ela precisa arranjar (have/get something done) ao se instalar.

Disponível no streaming: Netflix

Podcast
BBC Learning English — Have something done: the causative (English Class)

Video da BBC English Class que ensina a estrutura causativa 'have something done' (I had my hair cut) para dizer que outra pessoa faz algo para voce.

Dica: descreva 3 servicos que voce contratou usando 'I had ... done'.

Watch on YouTube ↗

YouTube
How To Leave the USA - 10 Steps to Moving Abroad

Video pratico de pessoas que se mudaram para o exterior, mostrando tudo que precisaram arranjar -- internet, SIM card, documentos traduzidos, casa pintada -- para se sentirem em casa. Revisao direta do vocabulario da aula.

Dica: pause o video e escreva 5 frases sobre o que voce teria feito (would have done) para se instalar num pais novo, usando a causativa.

Assistir no YouTube ↗

The Dream Key Words The Code Getting There Practice Your Turn Wrap-Up
Lesson 1 -- Travel English

Who Is
Pricila Adamo?

B1+ -- 60 minutes -- Alumni by Better

Chapter 1: The Dream

Let's Talk About You

"Tell me about your life in Araras."
"What do you enjoy doing in your free time?"
Chapter 1: The Dream

Your English Journey

"How long have you been studying English?"
"What is the hardest part of speaking English for you?"
"If your English were perfect tomorrow, what would you do first?"
Our Promise

"I want to be like a passport — always ready."

From "I know what I want to say but I cannot say it" to "passport ready, English ready" — in 40 lessons.

Chapter 2: Packing Words

Your Personal
Vocabulary

Ten essential words to tell your story in English

Vocabulary

Words 1-4

Something that happens to you that affects how you feel
Experience
Something that happens to you that affects how you feel or think
"I have had many experiences traveling."
A long trip, or a process of personal change
Journey
A long trip, or a process of personal change
"Learning English has been a long journey."
Able to speak a language easily and well
Fluent
Able to speak a language easily and well
"I want to become fluent before my trip."
Feeling sure about your own abilities
Confident
Feeling sure about your own abilities
"I feel more confident when I practice."
0 / 4 words revealed
Vocabulary

Words 5-8

To find something difficult; to have trouble
Struggle
To find something difficult; to have trouble doing something
"I struggle to find the right words."
A regular way of doing things; a fixed pattern
Routine
A regular way of doing things; a fixed pattern
"I make English part of my daily routine."
In or to a foreign country
Abroad
In or to a foreign country
"I have traveled abroad many times."
To stop working, usually because of your age
Retire
To stop working, usually because of your age
"I am going to retire soon."
0 / 4 words revealed
Vocabulary

Words 9-10

To succeed in doing something difficult
Accomplish
To succeed in doing something difficult
"I want to accomplish my dream."
To travel around a place to learn about it
Explore
To travel around a place to learn about it
"I love to explore new places."
0 / 2 words revealed
Review

Say It All

Click each word to listen. Repeat each one twice.

Experience"I have had many experiences."
Journey"It has been a long journey."
Fluent"I want to be fluent."
Confident"I feel more confident now."
Struggle"I struggle to find the words."
Routine"English is part of my routine."
Abroad"I have traveled abroad."
Retire"I am going to retire soon."
Accomplish"I want to accomplish my dream."
Explore"I love to explore new places."
Key Expressions

Your Story Starters

"I have been studying English for..."
Use this to talk about duration until now
"I grew up in..."
Use this to talk about your childhood or origins
"I have always wanted to..."
Use this to talk about lifelong dreams
"Right now, I am..."
Use this to talk about your current situation
"To be honest, the thing is..."
Use this to share something personal or honest
Chapter 3: The Code

Grammar
Discovery

Present Simple vs Present Perfect — what is the difference?

Grammar Discovery

Spot the Pattern

Look at the highlighted words. What do they have in common?

"I live in Araras." (now, always true)
"She goes to her clinic every day." (routine)
"I have traveled to many countries." (life experience)
"She has been studying English since 2000." (started then, continues now)
TenseUseSignal
Present Simplefacts, habits, routinesevery day, always, usually
Present Perfectexperiences, life achievementshave/has + past participle
Present Perfect Continuousactions that started in the past and continuehave/has been + verb-ing
Common Mistake

Watch Out!

"I study English since 2000."
"I have been studying English since 2000."

Use Present Perfect with "since" and "for" to connect the past to NOW.

Grammar Practice

Complete the Sentences

"I ___live in Araras." (fact — present simple)
"She ___has worked as a dentist for 25 years." (duration — present perfect)
"I ___have always wanted to visit Australia." (lifelong dream — present perfect)
"She ___goes to São Paulo every two weeks." (routine — present simple)
"I ___have been studying English since 2000." (continuous duration — present perfect continuous)
Oral Practice

Your Turn to Speak

Answer each question out loud. Then click to see a model answer.

"Where do you live?" (fact)
"I live in Araras, a small city in São Paulo state."
"How long have you been a dentist?" (duration)
"I have been a dentist for over twenty-five years."
"What countries have you visited?" (experiences)
"I have traveled to Canada, Malta, and many other countries."
"What do you do every day?" (routine)
"I go to my clinic and treat patients. I also try to practice English."
"What is your dream?" (lifelong wish)
"I have always wanted to be fluent in English and explore Australia."
"How long have you studied English?" (continuous duration)
"I have been studying English since the year 2000 — over twenty years."
Chapter 4: Getting There

A Conversation
at the Airport

Pricila meets David, an Australian, at the airport lounge

Dialogue

Airport Lounge

D
"Hi there! Are you flying to Sydney too?"
P
"Yes! This is going to be an amazing journey."
D
"What brings you to Australia?"
P
"I have always wanted to explore the country. I am a retired dentist from Brazil."
D
"That sounds wonderful! Have you been to Australia before?"
P
"No, this is my first time. I have traveled to many countries, but never to Australia."
D
"What do you want to accomplish on this trip?"
P
"I want to become more confident speaking English abroad. I have been studying for years."
Comprehension

Check Your Understanding

1. Where is Pricila flying to?
She is flying to Sydney, Australia.
2. What is Pricila's profession?
She is a retired dentist from Brazil.
3. Has Pricila been to Australia before?
No, this is her first time. But she has traveled to many other countries.
4. What does Pricila want to accomplish?
She wants to become more confident speaking English abroad.
Listening

Listen First

Listen to Pricila telling her story. Do NOT read — just listen.

0:000:00
Speed
Where did Pricila grow up?
She grew up in Araras, São Paulo.
How long has she been studying English?
She has been studying English since 2000 — over twenty years.
Real World

Your Boarding Pass

Alumni Airlines

BOARDING PASS
ADAMO, PRICILA
GRU - São Paulo
SYD - Sydney
AL 2025
B12
14A - Window
10:30 PM
Economy
Passport Ready. English Ready.
Listening 2

David Talks About Australia

Listen to David describing his country. What can you understand?

0:000:00
Speed
Where is David from?
He is from Sydney, Australia.
What does David recommend?
He recommends exploring the Great Barrier Reef and visiting Melbourne.
Chapter 5: Practice

Time to
Practice

Quick challenges, spot the error, and sentence building

Quick Fire

Quick Challenge

0 / 6
Question 1 of 6
A fellow traveler asks: "What do you do?" Introduce yourself.
"I am a dentist from Brazil. I have been working for over twenty-five years, but I am retiring soon. I love to explore new places."
Spot the Error

Find the Mistake

"I have study English for many years."
"I have studied English for many years." (have + past participle)
"She retire last year from her clinic."
"She retired last year from her clinic." (past simple for completed action)
"I am live in Araras since 2000."
"I have lived in Araras since 2000." (present perfect with since)
"He has went to Australia twice."
"He has gone to Australia twice." (gone = past participle of go)
0 / 4 errors found
Sentence Building

Build the Sentence

Words: I / have / English / studying / been / years / for / many
"I have been studying English for many years."
Words: She / traveled / abroad / has / many / times
"She has traveled abroad many times."
Words: I / to / always / wanted / explore / Australia / have
"I have always wanted to explore Australia."
Words: routine / English / my / part / daily / I / make / of
"I make English part of my daily routine."
Words: confident / more / when / I / I / practice / feel
"I feel more confident when I practice."
Oral Drilling

Respond in English

Teacher reads the situation. You respond in English.

Situation: Someone asks where you are from.
"I am from Araras, a small city in São Paulo state, Brazil."
Situation: Someone asks what you do for a living.
"I am a dentist, but I am retiring soon. I have worked for over twenty-five years."
Situation: Someone asks about your English learning history.
"I have been studying English since 2000. It has been a long journey, but I have never given up."
Situation: Someone asks about your travel dreams.
"I have always wanted to explore Australia. I want to accomplish my dream of being fluent and confident abroad."
Situation: Someone asks what is hardest about learning English.
"To be honest, the thing is I sometimes struggle to find the right words. But I feel more confident when I practice."
Situation: Someone asks about your daily routine with English.
"I try to make English part of my daily routine. I watch movies in English and keep my phone in English."
Chapter 6: Your Turn

Your Turn
to Speak

Three role-plays: guided, semi-free, and completely free

Guided Role-Play

Level 1: With Keywords

You are at an airport lounge in São Paulo. David, an Australian man, sits next to you and starts a conversation. Introduce yourself and tell him about your life, your career, and your English journey. Use the keywords below.
Experience Journey Fluent Confident Struggle Abroad Retire Accomplish Explore
Semi-Free Role-Play

Level 2: Sentence Starters

Continue the conversation with David. Use these sentence starters to help you:
"I am a... but I am..."
"I have been studying... for..."
"I have always wanted to..."
"To be honest, the thing is..."
"I want to accomplish..."
Free Role-Play

Level 3: Speak Freely

No keywords. No sentence starters. Just you and David having a real conversation at the airport. Talk about anything: your life, your career, your travels, your dreams, your struggles with English. Be yourself — in English.
Reflection

Record Your Story

Tell David one more thing about yourself that you have not mentioned yet. Who is the real Pricila Adamo?
Error Correction

Let's Polish

Teacher shares 3-5 errors from the free production. For each: what you said vs what to say.

What you said:
(Teacher writes here during class)
Better version:
(Teacher writes correction here)
What you said:
(Teacher writes here during class)
Better version:
(Teacher writes correction here)
What you said:
(Teacher writes here during class)
Better version:
(Teacher writes correction here)
Chapter 7: Wrap-Up

Wrapping
Up

Your survival card, checklist, and celebration

Survival Card

Pricila's Emergency Card

1
"Could you repeat that, please?"
2
"I am not sure I understand. Could you explain?"
3
"Let me think about that for a moment."
4
"To be honest, I have always wanted to..."
5
"I have been studying English for many years."
What I Learned

Lesson 1 Checklist

I can introduce myself in English
I can use Present Simple for facts and routines
I can use Present Perfect for life experiences
I can use Present Perfect Continuous for duration
I know 10 new vocabulary words
I can use 5 story starter expressions
I can have a conversation about myself at an airport
I can spot errors in Present Simple vs Present Perfect
I know my 5 survival phrases by heart
I feel more confident about telling my story in English

Day 1 — Complete!

Pricila Adamo told her story in English today.
Passport ready. English ready. 1 of 40.
